          "response_format_name": null,
          "text": "for n in range(1,13):\n    title, chunk = get_chapter_chunk(n)\n    print(\"\\n====== CH\",n, title,\"======\")\n    # print key lines summary quickly\n    # show bullet list and key beats maybe\n    print(\"\\n\".join(chunk.splitlines()[:18]))\n"

        "content": {
          "content_type": "execution_output",
          "text": "\n==== CH 3 ====\n### **CHAPTER 3: \"The Sky Breaks\"**\n- **POV:** Jhace (tight third)\n- **Tide:** 0, Striking (Piercing event)\n- **Setting:** Underface tunnel, then street level at building support\n- **Beat Method:** BEAT_03 (Crisis Front-Loaded → Rolling Complications → Partial Resolution)\n- **Saga Beat:** Beat 3\n- **Emotional Intensity:** High\n- **Word Count:** 2,200 words\n- **Status:** 🔒 LOCKED\n\n**Summary:**\n\n**Key Beats:**\n- Opens mid-catastrophe (wall moves before Jhace does)\n- Chloen trapped, Jhace's trauma activated (23 names callback)\n- First Soulpulse: contact + desperate need = copper-gold glow\n- Side-effect healing shown but not explained\n- Surface escape, white line visible overhead\n- Second Soulpulse on building support\n- Cost escalation: tremor → vein-lines → collapse\n- J/T meeting: analytical demand vs. exhausted answer\n\n**Soulpulse:** 2 uses (beam hold, building support)\n- Requirements confirmed: skin contact + desperate protective emotion\n- Visuals: copper-gold glow, spreads into material, traces stress lines\n- Cost: heat in palms during, tremor/vein-lines/pain/exhaustion after\n\n**Relationship:** First meeting through competence recognition (she measures, he builds)\n\n**Stage Progression:** 0 → 1 onset (tremor baseline establishing)\n\n---\n\n\n\n==== CH 4 ====\n### **CHAPTER 4: \"After the Shudder\"**\n- **POV:** Jhace (tight third)\n- **Tide:** 1, Kindling (morning after Piercing)\n- **Setting:** Triage chamber, Underface tunnels, kiln street\n- **Beat Method:** BEAT_02 (Trial → Failure → Compounding → Success/Cost)\n- **Saga Beat:** Beat 1\n- **Emotional Intensity:** Medium\n- **Word Count:** 2,350 words\n- **Status:** 🔒 LOCKED\n\n**Summary:**\n\n**Key Beats:**\n- Wakes to baseline tremor (Stage 1 established)\n- Chloen's healing confirmed (side-effect established)\n- Power test on non-critical crack: FAILS (conditional activation shown)\n- Emergency at triage wall: SUCCEEDS (immediate threat required)\n- Cost progression: worse tremor, can't grip nail (functional loss)\n- J/T dynamic: reluctant compliance because her logic is sound\n- Exit: \"The wall held. Jhace's grip did not.\"\n\n**Soulpulse:** 1 successful use (triage wall), 1 failed attempt (cracked support)\n- Conditional activation confirmed: requires immediate protective need\n- Cost accumulation shown: Stage 1 baseline tremor, fine motor compromised\n\n\n**Stage Progression:** Stage 1 baseline established, functional loss beginning\n\n---\n\n\n\n==== CH 5 ====\n### **CHAPTER 5: \"Lines That Don't Lie\"**\n- **Tide:** 2-3 (days after Piercing)\n- **Setting:** Kiln corridor streets, various hold sites\n- **Beat Method:** BEAT_01 (Observation → Pattern Recognition)\n- **Saga Beat:** Beat 10\n- **Emotional Intensity:** Medium\n- **Word Count:** 2,100 words\n- **Status:** 🔒 LOCKED\n\n**Summary:**\n\n**Key Beats:**\n- Opens with systematic mapping (investigation methodology)\n- RRO pressure: refuses institutional co-option\n- Guild pressure: refuses profitable retreat\n- Marisse callback: guilt drives street-level choice\n- Pattern documentation: conditional activation, cost progression\n- Pella support: practical care without coddling\n- Observes Jhace's decline from distance\n- Decision: engage to anchor stories with data before they kill him\n\n**Soulpulse:** None (observation chapter)\n\n\n**Stage Progression:** External observation of Stage 1 effects\n\n---\n\n\n\n==== CH 6 ====\n### **CHAPTER 6: \"Weight That Won't Yield\"**\n- **POV:** Jhace (tight third)\n- **Tide:** 3-4\n- **Setting:** Major structural failure at Saint Merro junction\n- **Beat Method:** BEAT_03 (Crisis Front-Loaded → Sustained Pressure → Narrow Success)\n- **Saga Beat:** Beat 3\n- **Emotional Intensity:** Medium-High\n- **Target Word Count:** ~1,800 words\n- **Status:** TO BE WRITTEN\n\n**Summary:**\n\n**Key Beats:**\n- Crisis opening: arch failing, people still inside\n- Jhace's traditional methods insufficient\n- First coordinated Soulpulse (she marks, he powers)\n- Success with visible cost increase\n- Her critique of his technique (care disguised as analysis)\n- His recognition: she sees him, not his heroism\n- Exit: they've choreographed perfectly without planning\n\n**Soulpulse:** 1 coordinated use (arch stabilization)\n- Demonstrates improved targeting reduces cost (her geometry + his power)\n- Still significant cost: tremor persistent, vein-lines linger\n\n**Relationship:** First true partnership action—coordination through shared competence\n\n**Stage Progression:** Stage 1 progressing, cost accumulation visible\n\n---\n\n\n\n==== CH 10 ====\n### **CHAPTER 10: \"Near Collapse\"**\n- **POV:** Both (alternating perspective within chapter)\n- **Tide:** 14-15\n- **Setting:** Major structural crisis at Saint Merro main support\n- **Beat Method:** BEAT_03 (External Threat → Narrow Escape)\n- **Emotional Intensity:** High\n- **Target Word Count:** ~1,800 words\n- **Status:** TO BE WRITTEN\n\n**Summary:**\n\n**Key Beats:**\n- Crisis: hundreds at risk, main support failing\n- Perfect coordination under pressure\n- Jhace pushes past limits (caretaker compulsion)\n- Success but near-Stage 2 cost\n- His rage: she took his choice\n- Her certainty: he would have killed himself\n- Relationship stress: care as control\n- Exit: silent fury and mutual recognition they need each other\n\n**Soulpulse:** 1 major use (near-Stage 2 threshold)\n- Coordinated efficiency maintains, but scale drives cost\n- Brief sensory overload (Stage 2 warning sign)\n- Vein-lines now faintly visible at rest\n\n**Relationship:** Conflict through care—she saves him by controlling him, he hates needing it\n\n**Stage Progression:** Stage 1 → approaching Stage 2 threshold (warning signs present)\n\n---\n\n\n"
        },

        "content": {
          "content_type": "code",
          "language": "unknown",
          "response_format_name": null,
          "text": "import os, re, textwrap, json, pandas as pd, numpy as np, pathlib, itertools, math\nfiles = [\n\"/mnt/data/Story_Bible_Template_Structure_v2_1.md\",\n\"/mnt/data/OMNIMETA_SAGA_META_CANON_MEMORY.md\",\n\"/mnt/data/50-Lexicon-Style.md\",\n\"/mnt/data/02-Soulpulse-Core.md\",\n\"/mnt/data/05-Knowledge-Gates-Discovery.md\",\n\"/mnt/data/10-World-TeOga.md\",\n\"/mnt/data/11-Factions-Politics.md\",\n\"/mnt/data/30-Turning1-All-Characters.md\",\n\"/mnt/data/N01_Beat_Mapping_Layer_FINAL.md\",\n\"/mnt/data/N01_Chapter_Grid_12ch_REVISED.md\",\n\"/mnt/data/03-Divine-Principles.md\",\n\"/mnt/data/04-Cosmology-Confluence.md\",\n\"/mnt/data/01-TeOga-Physical-Piercing.md\",\n\"/mnt/data/README.md\",\n\"/mnt/data/CUSTOM_GPT_SETUP.md\"\n]\ncontents={}\nfor f in files:\n    with open(f,'r',encoding='utf-8') as fp:\n        contents[os.path.basename(f)] = fp.read()\n{fn: len(txt) for fn,txt in contents.items()}"
        },

        "end_turn": false,

          "text": "### Jhace Torrin (Full Coverage in N01â€“N05 Rewritten Bibles)\n\n**N01 Arc:** Stranger â†’ Partner â†’ Lover (tremor-bound)  \n**N02 Arc:** Partner â†’ Co-leader â†’ Revolutionary  \n**N03â€“N05 Arc:** (To be detailed below)\n\n---\n\n\n**N01 Arc:** Stranger â†’ Partner â†’ Lover (precision-bound)  \n**N02 Arc:** Partner â†’ Co-leader â†’ Sacrificer  \n**N03â€“N05 Arc:** (To be detailed below)\n\n---\n\n## TIER 2: PRIMARY SUPPORTS\n\n\n"
        },
        "create_time": 1765789923.5182004,
        "end_turn": null,
        "id": "1957a62c-f3b6-4be7-af4e-5a2f930a16bf",
        "metadata": {
          "aggregate_result": {
            "code": "def extract_section(text, start_heading):\n    # extract from heading line to next ### heading\n    pattern = re.escape(\"### \"+start_heading)\n    m=re.search(pattern, text)\n    if not m: return \"\"\n    start=m.start()\n    # find next heading after start\n    m2=re.search(r\"^### \", text[m.end():], flags=re.M)\n    end = m.end()+m2.start() if m2 else len(text)\n    return text[start:end]\njhace_section = extract_section(chars_doc, headings[0])\nprint(jhace_section[:2500])\n",
            "end_time": 1765789923.5996215,
            "final_expression_output": null,
            "in_kernel_exception": null,
            "jupyter_messages": [
              {
                "content": {

          "text": "### **CHAPTER 12: \"Choosing the Weight\"**\n- **POV:** Both (balanced perspective)\n- **Tide:** 19-20\n- **Setting:** Rebuilt overlook above Saint Merro, forge wall\n- **Beat Method:** BEAT_21 (Rest → Reflection → Renewed Purpose)\n- **Emotional Intensity:** Medium\n- **Target Word Count:** ~1,600 words\n- **Status:** TO BE WRITTEN\n\n**Summary:**\n\n**Key Beats:**\n- Walk through rebuilt district (uneven success)\n- Recognition of larger forces gathering\n- Decision: focus on what they can hold\n- Three priorities written on forge wall\n- Community witnesses/joins commitment\n- Shared physical moment (tremor through linked hands)\n- Acceptance: this is who they are together\n- Exit: foundation locked, future uncertain but faced together\n\n**Soulpulse:** None (commitment/resolution chapter)\n\n**Relationship:** Partnership formalized through shared principles (not romance, but deeper)\n\n**Stage Progression:** Stage 1 baseline accepted as new permanent state\n\n---\n\n- **Word Count Target:** 800-1,000 words\n- **Status:** TO BE WRITTEN\n\n**Summary:**\n\n**Key Elements:**\n- Meta-perspective assessment\n- Character analysis (unpredictability as threat/asset)\n- Bond recognition (functional love, not romantic)\n- Prediction of institutional response\n- Turning 1 trajectory noted\n- Setup for N02 external pressure\n\n\n---\n\n## INTERLUDE PLACEMENT SUMMARY\n\n- **Interlude I:** After Chapter 7 (mid-Act II, after partnership forms)\n- **Interlude II:** After Chapter 10 (between crisis and resolution, shows divine forces gathering)\n- **Positioning Logic:** Interludes provide breathing space after intense chapters, offer divine/meta perspective, foreshadow external pressures coming in N02\n\n---\n\n## POV DISTRIBUTION (12 CHAPTERS)\n\n- **Jhace Solo:** 5 chapters (1, 3, 4, 6, 8)\n- **Both/Shared:** 3 chapters (10, 11, 12)\n- **Other (Interludes/Epilogue):** 3 segments\n\n**Pattern:** Opens with alternating solo POVs (establishes both independently), moves toward shared POVs as partnership deepens (reflects relationship progression)\n\n---\n\n## BEAT METHOD DISTRIBUTION\n\n**Opening Exception:** BEAT_01 → BEAT_01 permitted for Ch 1–2 only to establish parallel baseline POVs through deliberate structural mirror.\n\n**Used in N01:**\n- BEAT_01: Ch 1, 2, 5 (Saga Beat 10)\n- BEAT_02: Ch 4 (Saga Beat 1)\n- BEAT_03: Ch 3, 6, 10 (Saga Beat 3)\n- BEAT_04: Ch 8 (Saga Beat 16)\n- BEAT_07: Ch 9 (Saga Beat 26)\n- BEAT_08: Ch 7, 11, 12 (Saga Beat 17 or 21 depending on intimacy vs. reflection emphasis)\n\n**Total Unique Beats:** 6 different N01 local methods across 12 chapters\n**Repeated Beats:** \n- BEAT_01 used 3x (Ch 1, 2, 5 - opening parallel\n"
        },

          "text": "### **INTERLUDE I: \"Corlexi - System vs Chaos\"**\n- **POV:** Corlexi (Form god-vessel, mortal name unknown to self)\n- **Tide:** ~6-7\n- **Setting:** Crown administrative chambers, multiple observation points\n- **Type:** Build-Up\n- **Word Count Target:** 600-900 words\n- **Status:** TO BE WRITTEN\n\n**Summary:**\n\n**Key Elements:**\n- Sensory overwhelm technique (god-vessel perception)\n- Principle bias manifestation (everything seen through Form lens)\n- Innocent frame maintained (no recognition of divinity)\n\n**Purpose:** Establish divine forces moving, show limitation of single-Principle perspective, foreshadow institutional pressure in Act III\n\n---\n\n\n"

        "content": {
          "content_type": "execution_output",
          "text": "### **CHAPTER 11: \"Holding Together\"**\n- **POV:** Both (intimate alternating perspective)\n- **Tide:** 17-18\n- **Beat Method:** BEAT_17 (Intimacy → Vulnerability → Connection)\n- **Emotional Intensity:** High\n- **Target Word Count:** ~1,900 words\n- **Status:** TO BE WRITTEN\n\n**Summary:**\n\n**Key Beats:**\n- Post-crisis exhaustion (defenses down)\n- Trauma exchange: his 23 names, her Marisse\n- Recognition: both atoning for unprevented deaths\n- Mutual need acknowledged (operational, not romantic)\n- Physical intimacy: first non-crisis contact\n- Commitment: she keeps him honest, he trusts her override\n- Exit: understanding they can't survive apart\n\n**Soulpulse:** None (recovery/intimacy chapter)\n\n**Relationship:** Trauma-bonding → mutual recognition of load-bearing function\n\n**Stage Progression:** Stage 1 recovering with rest, but baseline effects permanent\n\n---\n\n\n"
        },

        "channel": null,
        "content": {
          "content_type": "execution_output",
          "text": "## Exit Point: Northeastern Hemisphere\n\n**Location:**\n- Land-based impact\n- Region containing Te'Oga's Crown (major city)\n- Saint Merro district (narrow leaning streets) experiences exit directly\n\n**Immediate Effects:**\n- Planet yanked from tilted axis to upright **as object exits**\n- Violent shaking (both rotational shift and exit shockwave)\n- Massive debris field: Outer ring of rubble (Te'Ogan rock + projectile residue)\n- Buildings collapse, fires spread, mass casualties\n\n**Exit Scar Geography:**\n- Open hole approximately 30 bars (~27 meters) wide at surface\n- Rubble ring extends several cables (~200-400 meters) outward\n- Thermally active zone (superheated rock, ongoing sublimation)\n\n\n---\n\n\n"
        },
        "create_time": 1765789982.6781433,

    "2ff2132c-8c1c-4d86-a3f2-670cca819c87": {

        "7c38ef87-4c3d-482a-81a9-8e1dfa9ae4c8"

      "message": {
        "author": {
          "metadata": {},
          "name": "python",
          "role": "tool"
        },
        "channel": null,
        "content": {
          "content_type": "execution_output",
          "text": "### **CHAPTER 5: \"Lines That Don't Lie\"**\n- **Tide:** 2-3 (days after Piercing)\n- **Setting:** Kiln corridor streets, various hold sites\n- **Beat Method:** BEAT_01 (Observation → Pattern Recognition)\n- **Saga Beat:** Beat 10\n- **Emotional Intensity:** Medium\n- **Word Count:** 2,100 words\n- **Status:** 🔒 LOCKED\n\n**Summary:**\n\n**Key Beats:**\n- Opens with systematic mapping (investigation methodology)\n- RRO pressure: refuses institutional co-option\n- Guild pressure: refuses profitable retreat\n- Marisse callback: guilt drives street-level choice\n- Pattern documentation: conditional activation, cost progression\n- Pella support: practical care without coddling\n- Observes Jhace's decline from distance\n- Decision: engage to anchor stories with data before they kill him\n\n**Soulpulse:** None (observation chapter)\n\n\n**Stage Progression:** External observation of Stage 1 effects\n\n---\n\n\n"
        },
        "create_time": 1765790332.9982538,
        "end_turn": null,
        "id": "2ff2132c-8c1c-4d86-a3f2-670cca819c87",
        "metadata": {
          "aggregate_result": {
            "code": "ch5txt=[p for p in parsed if p['kind']==\"CHAPTER\" and p['num']==5][0]['text']\nprint(ch5txt[:2500])\n",
            "end_time": 1765790333.0720308,
            "final_expression_output": null,
            "in_kernel_exception": null,
            "jupyter_messages": [
              {
                "content": {

        "channel": null,
        "content": {
          "content_type": "execution_output",
          "text": "### 3.1 Teâ€™Ogaâ€™s Crown & Saint Merro (Exit-Side City)\n\n**Location & Layout**\n\n- Teâ€™Ogaâ€™s Crown is a major mid-latitude port and political center on the northeastern hemisphere, built along a layered harbor with stacked districts.  \n- The city tiers:\n  - **The Crown District:** Hilltop civic and guild halls; newer stonework, broader streets.  \n  - **Saint Merro:** Narrow, leaning brick streets below, older load-bearing walls, marginal foundationsâ€”hit hardest by the exit event.  \n  - **The Underface:** Sub-street level of arches, culverts, and maintenance corridors; Jhaceâ€™s forge is here.\n\n**Post-Piercing Condition**\n\n- Exit scar crater and rubble ring sit just beyond the Crownâ€™s urban line:\n  - Crater: ~50 cables across at the rim, thermally active, part of it visible from high buildings.  \n  - Rubble ring: unstable stone and fused glass/metal, partly quarantined by guild decree.  \n- Saint Merro: Many buildings leaning or collapsed after quakes; improvised shoring, rope stays, and emergency braces everywhere.\n\n**Role in Saga**\n\n- Primary setting for N01â€“N05:\n  - Jhaceâ€™s forge (Underface, two cables below main streets).  \n- Later Turnings: Becomes symbol of â€œwhat can be heldâ€ after repeated stabilization and reconstruction attempts.\n\n---\n\n### 3.2 Entry-Side Coast & Harbor City (Mobelâ€™s Ground)\n\n**Location & Layout**\n\n- On the opposite hemisphere, near the northwestern coast where the spindle entered through the ocean.  \n- Key city: **Harbor of First Sound** (working name; can be refined), a tidal port built along steep cliffs and narrow inlets.\n\n**Post-Piercing Features**\n\n- Entry scar: Bottomless hole in the sea floor, several cables off the coast; marked by permanent whirlpools and steam vents.  \n- Surrounding waters:\n  - Dangerous currents and upwellings; whole fishing patterns changed.  \n  - Declared forbidden by Church/Guild edicts, but smugglers still test the edges.\n\n**Role in Saga**\n\n- Mobelâ€™s home ground and early-stage workplace:\n  - Harbor pilot, tide reader, and later Purity-aligned divine mask.  \n- Entry-side doctrine frames the Piercing as a â€œcleansing strikeâ€; local culture leans toward Purity rhetoric.\n\n---\n\n### 3.3 Southern Communes (Wholeness Belt)\n\n**Location & Layout**\n\n- South of Teâ€™Ogaâ€™s Crown, across a few reaches of mixed farmland and low hills.  \n- A network of agrarian communes and trade hamlets rather than single megacities.\n\n**Culture & Feel**\n\n- Emphasis on mutual aid, shared labor, and communal \n"
        },

    "36027a03-b59c-4739-9568-38eb49036ce5": {
      "children": [
        "010e1017-82b4-411c-a096-97d1fe3b2266"
      ],
      "id": "36027a03-b59c-4739-9568-38eb49036ce5",
      "message": {
        "author": {
          "metadata": {},
          "name": "python",
          "role": "tool"
        },
        "channel": null,
        "content": {
          "content_type": "execution_output",
          "text": "# STORY BIBLE GENERATION TEMPLATE\n## OmniMeta Saga: Mechanical Production System for N06-N30\n\n**Version:** 2.1 (12-Chapter Structure)  \n**Last Updated:** December 2025  \n**Purpose:** Formalized template structure for automated, constraint-compliant bible generation\n\n---\n\n## TEMPLATE OVERVIEW\n\nThis template defines the **14 mandatory sections** for every OmniMeta novella bible. Each section is classified by update type:\n\n**Novella Structure (12-Chapter Standard):**\n- **12 numbered chapters** (narrative spine, 1,200-2,200 words each)\n- **2 interludes** (god-vessel POV, placed between chapters)\n- **= 15 total narrative segments**\n\nEach section is classified by update type:\n\n- **[FIXED]** = Never changes across novellas (locked canon)\n- **[VARIABLE]** = Unique per novella (generated fresh each time)\n- **[CUMULATIVE]** = Builds on previous novellas (growing context)\n- **[CHECKPOINT]** = Quality assurance verification gate\n\n---\n\n## SECTION 0: NOVELLA PARAMETERS [VARIABLE]\n\n### Required Fields\n\n```\n- Novella ID: [N##]\n- Working Title: [Title - First letter locked to acrostic]\n- Turning: [#] â€“ [METAPHOR WORD] (Metaphor description)\n- Acrostic Letter: [Letter] in [WORD]\n- Acrostic Position: [#/5] in Turning [#]\n- Word Count Target: ~18,000-21,000 (12 chapters + 2 interludes + 1 epilogue)\n- Chapter Word Count: 1,200-2,200 words each (variance permitted for beat requirements)\n- Timeline Covered: Tide [##] through Tide [##]\n- Saga Position: Novella [##] of 30\n- Knowledge Frame: [INNOCENT/GUILTY]\n```\n\n### Bible Version History\n\n```\n- v1.0-DRAFT: [Date] - Initial generation\n- v1.1-VERIFIED: [Date] - Passed all checkpoints\n- v2.0-COMPLETE: [Date] - Approved for prose generation\n- v2.1-LOCKED: [Date] - Prose complete, archival version\n```\n\n### Acrostic Verification Checkpoint âœ“\n\n**Verification Required:**\n- [ ] Title first letter matches turning acrostic position\n- [ ] Turning metaphor correctly identified\n- [ ] Position in saga correctly numbered\n\n---\n\n## SECTION 1: SAGA STRUC\n"
        },
        "create_time": 1765789873.9069264,
        "end_turn": null,
        "id": "36027a03-b59c-4739-9568-38eb49036ce5",
        "metadata": {
          "aggregate_result": {
            "code": "template = contents[\"Story_Bible_Template_Structure_v2_1.md\"]\nprint(template[:2000])\n",
            "end_time": 1765789873.9789062,
            "final_expression_output": null,
            "in_kernel_exception": null,
            "jupyter_messages": [
              {
                "content": {
                  "execution_state": "busy"
                },
                "msg_type": "status",
                "parent_header": {
                  "msg_id": "3077b3ef-8ea9dd42307b327ffc80409f_3_1",
                  "version": "5.3"
                }
              },
              {
                "msg_type": "execute_input",
                "parent_header": {
                  "msg_id": "3077b3ef-8ea9dd42307b327ffc80409f_3_1",
                  "version": "5.3"
                }
              },
              {
                "content": {
                  "name": "stdout",
                  "text": "# STORY BIBLE GENERATION TEMPLATE\n## OmniMeta Saga: Mechanical Production System for N06-N30\n\n**Version:** 2.1 (12-Chapter Structure)  \n**Last Updated:** December 2025  \n**Purpose:** Formalized template structure for automated, constraint-compliant bible generation\n\n---\n\n## TEMPLATE OVERVIEW\n\nThis template defines the **14 mandatory sections** for every OmniMeta novella bible. Each section is classified by update type:\n\n**Novella Structure (12-Chapter Standard):**\n- **12 numbered chapters** (narrative spine, 1,200-2,200 words each)\n- **2 interludes** (god-vessel POV, placed between chapters)\n- **= 15 total narrative segments**\n\nEach section is classified by update type:\n\n- **[FIXED]** = Never changes across novellas (locked canon)\n- **[VARIABLE]** = Unique per novella (generated fresh each time)\n- **[CUMULATIVE]** = Builds on previous novellas (growing context)\n- **[CHECKPOINT]** = Quality assurance verification gate\n\n---\n\n## SECTION 0: NOVELLA PARAMETERS [VARIABLE]\n\n### Required Fields\n\n```\n- Novella ID: [N##]\n- Working Title: [Title - First letter locked to acrostic]\n- Turning: [#] â€“ [METAPHOR WORD] (Metaphor description)\n- Acrostic Letter: [Letter] in [WORD]\n- Acrostic Position: [#/5] in Turning [#]\n- Word Count Target: ~18,000-21,000 (12 chapters + 2 interludes + 1 epilogue)\n- Chapter Word Count: 1,200-2,200 words each (variance permitted for beat requirements)\n- Timeline Covered: Tide [##] through Tide [##]\n- Saga Position: Novella [##] of 30\n- Knowledge Frame: [INNOCENT/GUILTY]\n```\n\n### Bible Version History\n\n```\n- v1.0-DRAFT: [Date] - Initial generation\n- v1.1-VERIFIED: [Date] - Passed all checkpoints\n- v2.0-COMPLETE: [Date] - Approved for prose generation\n- v2.1-LOCKED: [Date] - Prose complete, archival version\n```\n\n### Acrostic Verification Checkpoint âœ“\n\n**Verification Required:**\n- [ ] Title first letter matches turning acrostic position\n- [ ] Turning metaphor correctly identified\n- [ ] Position in saga correctly numbered\n\n---\n\n## SECTION 1: SAGA STRUC\n"
                },
                "msg_type": "stream",
                "parent_header": {

        "content": {
          "content_type": "execution_output",
          "text": "### **CHAPTER 10: \"Near Collapse\"**\n- **POV:** Both (alternating perspective within chapter)\n- **Tide:** 14-15\n- **Setting:** Major structural crisis at Saint Merro main support\n- **Beat Method:** BEAT_03 (External Threat → Narrow Escape)\n- **Emotional Intensity:** High\n- **Target Word Count:** ~1,800 words\n- **Status:** TO BE WRITTEN\n\n**Summary:**\n\n**Key Beats:**\n- Crisis: hundreds at risk, main support failing\n- Perfect coordination under pressure\n- Jhace pushes past limits (caretaker compulsion)\n- Success but near-Stage 2 cost\n- His rage: she took his choice\n- Her certainty: he would have killed himself\n- Relationship stress: care as control\n- Exit: silent fury and mutual recognition they need each other\n\n**Soulpulse:** 1 major use (near-Stage 2 threshold)\n- Coordinated efficiency maintains, but scale drives cost\n- Brief sensory overload (Stage 2 warning sign)\n- Vein-lines now faintly visible at rest\n\n**Relationship:** Conflict through care—she saves him by controlling him, he hates needing it\n\n**Stage Progression:** Stage 1 → approaching Stage 2 threshold (warning signs present)\n\n---\n\n\n"
        },
        "create_time": 1765790341.0403593,
        "end_turn": null,

        "channel": null,
        "content": {
          "content_type": "execution_output",
          "text": "\n**Stage Progression:** Stage 1 baseline accepted as new permanent state\n\n---\n\n- **Word Count Target:** 800-1,000 words\n- **Status:** TO BE WRITTEN\n\n**Summary:**\n\n**Key Elements:**\n- Meta-perspective assessment\n- Character analysis (unpredictability as threat/asset)\n- Bond recognition (functional love, not romantic)\n- Prediction of institutional response\n- Turning 1 trajectory noted\n- Setup for N02 external pressure\n\n\n---\n\n## INTERLUDE PLACEMENT SUMMARY\n\n- **Interlude I:** After Chapter 7 (mid-Act II, after partnership forms)\n- **Interlude II:** After Chapter 10 (between crisis and resolution, shows divine forces gathering)\n- **Positioning Logic:** Interludes provide breathing space after intense chapters, offer divine/meta perspective, foreshadow external pressures coming in N02\n\n---\n\n## POV DISTRIBUTION (12 CHAPTERS)\n\n- **Jhace Solo:** 5 chapters (1, 3, 4, 6, 8)\n- **Both/Shared:** 3 chapters (10, 11, 12)\n- **Other (Interludes/Epilogue):** 3 segments\n\n**Pattern:** Opens with alternating solo POVs (establishes both independently), moves toward shared POVs as partnership deepens (reflects relationship progression)\n\n---\n\n## BEAT METHOD DISTRIBUTION\n\n**Opening Exception:** BEAT_01 → BEAT_01 permitted for Ch 1–2 only to establish parallel baseline POVs through deliberate structural mirror.\n\n**Used in N01:**\n- BEAT_01: Ch 1, 2, 5 (Saga Beat 10)\n- BEAT_02: Ch 4 (Saga Beat 1)\n- BEAT_03: Ch 3, 6, 10 (Saga Beat 3)\n- BEAT_04: Ch 8 (Saga Beat 16)\n- BEAT_07: Ch 9 (Saga Beat 26)\n- BEAT_08: Ch 7, 11, 12 (Saga Beat 17 or 21 depending on intimacy vs. reflection emphasis)\n\n**Total Unique Beats:** 6 different N01 local methods across 12 chapters\n**Repeated Beats:** \n- BEAT_01 used 3x (Ch 1, 2, 5 - opening parallel exception + later observation)\n- BEAT_03 used 3x (Ch 3, 6, 10 - crisis-heavy novella, acceptable)\n- BEAT_08 used 3x (Ch 7, 11, 12 - recovery/intimacy/commitment arc)\n\n---\n\n## EMOTIONAL INTENSITY MAP\n\n**Chapter Intensity Distribution:**\n- **High (4 chapters):** 3, 10, 11, 12\n- **Medium (6 chapters):** 4, 5, 6, 8, 9, 12\n- **Low (2 chapters):** 1, 2, 7\n\n**Arc Pattern:** Low opening → High crisis → Medium stabilization → High final crisis → Medium resolution\n\n---\n\n## SOULPULSE USAGE TRACKING\n\n**Total Uses in N01:** 7 confirmed uses\n- Ch 3: 2 uses (beam, building support)\n- Ch 4: 1 use (triage wall) + 1 failed attempt\n- Ch 6: 1 use (arch coordination)\n- Ch 8: 1 use (efficiency test)\n- Ch 10: 1 use (main support, near-Stage 2)\n\n**Stage Progression:**\n"
        },

                "text": "\n**Stage Progression:** Stage 1 baseline accepted as new permanent state\n\n---\n\n- **Word Count Target:** 800-1,000 words\n- **Status:** TO BE WRITTEN\n\n**Summary:**\n\n**Key Elements:**\n- Meta-perspective assessment\n- Character analysis (unpredictability as threat/asset)\n- Bond recognition (functional love, not romantic)\n- Prediction of institutional response\n- Turning 1 trajectory noted\n- Setup for N02 external pressure\n\n\n---\n\n## INTERLUDE PLACEMENT SUMMARY\n\n- **Interlude I:** After Chapter 7 (mid-Act II, after partnership forms)\n- **Interlude II:** After Chapter 10 (between crisis and resolution, shows divine forces gathering)\n- **Positioning Logic:** Interludes provide breathing space after intense chapters, offer divine/meta perspective, foreshadow external pressures coming in N02\n\n---\n\n## POV DISTRIBUTION (12 CHAPTERS)\n\n- **Jhace Solo:** 5 chapters (1, 3, 4, 6, 8)\n- **Both/Shared:** 3 chapters (10, 11, 12)\n- **Other (Interludes/Epilogue):** 3 segments\n\n**Pattern:** Opens with alternating solo POVs (establishes both independently), moves toward shared POVs as partnership deepens (reflects relationship progression)\n\n---\n\n## BEAT METHOD DISTRIBUTION\n\n**Opening Exception:** BEAT_01 → BEAT_01 permitted for Ch 1–2 only to establish parallel baseline POVs through deliberate structural mirror.\n\n**Used in N01:**\n- BEAT_01: Ch 1, 2, 5 (Saga Beat 10)\n- BEAT_02: Ch 4 (Saga Beat 1)\n- BEAT_03: Ch 3, 6, 10 (Saga Beat 3)\n- BEAT_04: Ch 8 (Saga Beat 16)\n- BEAT_07: Ch 9 (Saga Beat 26)\n- BEAT_08: Ch 7, 11, 12 (Saga Beat 17 or 21 depending on intimacy vs. reflection emphasis)\n\n**Total Unique Beats:** 6 different N01 local methods across 12 chapters\n**Repeated Beats:** \n- BEAT_01 used 3x (Ch 1, 2, 5 - opening parallel exception + later observation)\n- BEAT_03 used 3x (Ch 3, 6, 10 - crisis-heavy novella, acceptable)\n- BEAT_08 used 3x (Ch 7, 11, 12 - recovery/intimacy/commitment arc)\n\n---\n\n## EMOTIONAL INTENSITY MAP\n\n**Chapter Intensity Distribution:**\n- **High (4 chapters):** 3, 10, 11, 12\n- **Medium (6 chapters):** 4, 5, 6, 8, 9, 12\n- **Low (2 chapters):** 1, 2, 7\n\n**Arc Pattern:** Low opening → High crisis → Medium stabilization → High final crisis → Medium resolution\n\n---\n\n## SOULPULSE USAGE TRACKING\n\n**Total Uses in N01:** 7 confirmed uses\n- Ch 3: 2 uses (beam, building support)\n- Ch 4: 1 use (triage wall) + 1 failed attempt\n- Ch 6: 1 use (arch coordination)\n- Ch 8: 1 use (efficiency test)\n- Ch 10: 1 use (main support, near-Stage 2)\n\n**Stage Progression:**\n",
                "time": 1765789938.8859434
              }
            ],

        "channel": null,
        "content": {
          "content_type": "execution_output",
          "text": " â€” gods and masks\n- `04-Cosmology-Confluence.md` â€” hidden truth\n- `00-Saga-Blueprint.md` â€” timeline and structure\n\n---\n\n## 1. KNOWLEDGE HIERARCHY\n\n### Universal Baseline (Everyone)\n\nBy start of N01, all Te'Oga citizens know:\n- A massive object pierced the planet\n- Earthquakes and tsunamis resulted\n- Axis tilted; seasons are gone\n- Something called \"Soulpulse\" or \"resonance\" is appearing in some people\n- It seems dangerous and unpredictable\n\n### Doctrine Baseline (Regional Variation)\n\nEvery region has different, often contradictory scripture about:\n- Whether gods once walked Te'Oga or are purely metaphor\n- Which Principle is holy/damned\n- Whether the Piercing was punishment, accident, or test\n\nAll doctrine contains fragments of truth mangled beyond recognition.\n\n### Specialized Knowledge Tiers\n\n- **Tier 1: Craft-Minded**: Instinctively understand structural stress and Soulpulse use\n- **Tier 2: Trained Users**: Can articulate theory, teach others, recognize Soulpulse signatures\n- *\n"
        },

        "content": {
          "content_type": "execution_output",
          "text": "### **CHAPTER 4: \"After the Shudder\"**\n- **POV:** Jhace (tight third)\n- **Tide:** 1, Kindling (morning after Piercing)\n- **Setting:** Triage chamber, Underface tunnels, kiln street\n- **Beat Method:** BEAT_02 (Trial → Failure → Compounding → Success/Cost)\n- **Saga Beat:** Beat 1\n- **Emotional Intensity:** Medium\n- **Word Count:** 2,350 words\n- **Status:** 🔒 LOCKED\n\n**Summary:**\n\n**Key Beats:**\n- Wakes to baseline tremor (Stage 1 established)\n- Chloen's healing confirmed (side-effect established)\n- Power test on non-critical crack: FAILS (conditional activation shown)\n- Emergency at triage wall: SUCCEEDS (immediate threat required)\n- Cost progression: worse tremor, can't grip nail (functional loss)\n- J/T dynamic: reluctant compliance because her logic is sound\n- Exit: \"The wall held. Jhace's grip did not.\"\n\n**Soulpulse:** 1 successful use (triage wall), 1 failed attempt (cracked support)\n- Conditional activation confirmed: requires immediate protective need\n- Cost accumulation shown: Stage 1 baseline tremor, fine motor compromised\n\n\n**Stage Progression:** Stage 1 baseline established, functional loss beginning\n\n---\n\n\n"
        },
        "create_time": 1765790319.8519826,
        "end_turn": null,

          "text": "# TURNING 1: COMPLETE CHARACTER ARCS\n**All Characters Introduced in N01â€“N05 (GLASS)**\n\n---\n\n## 0. CHARACTER TIER SYSTEM\n\n- **Tier 2 (Primary Supports):** Bram, Corvin, Tellis, Ressa, Chloen (multiple chapters, deep internal moments)\n- **Tier 3 (Faction Anchors):** Church representatives, RRO officials, guild factors (recurring, ideological weight)\n- **Tier 4 (Scene-Specific):** Refugees, workers, black-market trainers, family members (single/few chapters, specific purpose)\n\n---\n\n## TIER 1: PROTAGONISTS\n\n### Jhace Torrin (Full Coverage in N01â€“N05 Rewritten Bibles)\n\n**N01 Arc:** Stranger â†’ Partner â†’ Lover (tremor-bound)  \n**N02 Arc:** Partner â†’ Co-leader â†’ Revolutionary  \n**N03â€“N05 Arc:** (To be detailed below)\n\n---\n\n\n**N01 Arc:** Stranger â†’ Partner â†’ Lover (precision-bound)  \n**N02 Arc:** Partner â†’ Co-leader â†’ Sacrificer  \n**N03â€“N05 Arc:** (To be detailed below)\n\n---\n\n## TIER 2: PRIMARY SUPPORTS\n\n### Bram Kethis\n\n**Role:** Jhace's mentor, moral anchor, witness to love story\n\n**Psychology:**\n- **Core Trait:** Practical wisdom disguised as gruffness\n- **Principle Bias:** Form/Wholeness hybrid (builder who cares about people)\n- **Internal Belief:** *Young people learn by making mistakes and being held accountable. My job is to hold the line.*\n\n**Character Arc (N01â€“N05):**\n\n**N01:**\n- Appears in Ch1 (teases Jhace about working alone)\n- Rescues Jhace from forge collapse (Ch3)\n- Offers practical support and space for partnership to form\n- **N01 Role:** Witness and enabler; he sees what is forming and approves\n\n**N02:**\n- Confronts Jhace about overextension (Ch11)\n- Warns him not to abandon the work that made him\n- Places hand on both their shoulders when they write the three priorities (Ch14)\n- Says: \"You are building a better city with each other. That is all any of us can do.\"\n- **N02 Role:** Anchor to craft and reality; reminds them what they are protecting\n\n**N03â€“N04:**\n- Increasing strain as Jhace becomes more public-facing\n- Develops a min\n"
        },

    "4f30047e-5da4-4bb5-9d00-e4d30f278676": {
      "children": [
        "60902eb4-7f54-447e-bba8-1870f9d45a8e"
      ],
      "id": "4f30047e-5da4-4bb5-9d00-e4d30f278676",
      "message": {
        "author": {
          "metadata": {},
          "name": "python",
          "role": "tool"
        },
        "channel": null,
        "content": {
          "content_type": "execution_output",
          "text": "### 7.1 Immediate Physical Cost (Four Stages)\n\nThe short-term cost ladder remains as previously defined, but now explicitly underpins higher-tier forms. [attached_file:10]  \n\n1. **Strain:** Minutes of light use; tremors, headache; recovers in minutes. [attached_file:10]  \n2. **Fatigue:** Extended use; shaking, irritability; recovers overnight. [attached_file:10]  \n3. **Burnout:** Heavy use; collapse risk; recovers over days with care. [attached_file:11]  \n4. **Collapse:** Extreme or Confluent use; unconsciousness; recovers over days to weeks if environment permits. [attached_file:11]  \n\n### 7.2 Long-Term Accrued Cost\n\nRepeated heavy use accumulates **structural wear** on the user: [attached_file:10]  \n\n- Nerve damage and misfires. [attached_file:10]  \n- Chronic pain and joint issues. [attached_file:12]  \n- Emotional blunting or empathy collapse (depending on Principle bias). [attached_file:12]  \n- Life expectancy reduction for Fathombreak-level or repeated high-tier users. [attached_file:11]  \n\n### 7.3 Indirect Fatality\n\nSoulpulse itself does not directly â€œburn outâ€ souls as a mystical execution. [attached_file:10]  \nPeople die because they collapse where buildings are falling, water is rising, or enemies are waiting. [attached_file:7]  \n\n\n---\n\n## 8. RANGE, DURATION, AND VISIBILITY\n\n### 8.1 Range\n\n- Touch: Strongest, most reliable; required for early Turning 1 use. [attached_file:9]  \n- Line-of-sight: Becomes common by late Turning 1 for naturals, more broadly by Turning 2. [attached_file:10]  \n- Through obstacles: Rare mastery achieved mid-saga for a few, requiring intimate knowledge of target structure. [attached_file:14]  \n\nMaximum ranges stay **human-scale** (tens of meters), but effects can propagate further indirectly through structures and systems. [attached_file:10]  \n\n### 8.2 Duration\n\n- Most effects require continuous mental â€œgripâ€ and end within breaths or moments after focus breaks. [attached_file:9]  \n- Some structural changes, once forced past elastic limits, persist (e.g., a beam bent straight and held long enough). [attached_file:10]  \n\n### 8.3 Visibility and Anime Escalation\n\n**Turning 1 (N01â€“N05):**  \n- No overt auras or energy beams; story stays grounded and close to material reality. [attached_file:7]  \n\n**Post-Fathombreak (N06+):**  \n- High-tier users and gods entering **Resonance Forms** begin to manifest visible overtones:  \n  - Air ripples, dust spirals, faint glows near hands or eyes under heavy load. [attached_file:14]  \n"
        },
        "create_time": 1765789966.4308112,
        "end_turn": null,
        "id": "4f30047e-5da4-4bb5-9d00-e4d30f278676",

        "content": {

          "text": "### Jhace Torrin (Full Coverage in N01â€“N05 Rewritten Bibles)\n\n**N01 Arc:** Stranger â†’ Partner â†’ Lover (tremor-bound)  \n**N02 Arc:** Partner â†’ Co-leader â†’ Revolutionary  \n**N03â€“N05 Arc:** (To be detailed below)\n\n---\n\n\n**N01 Arc:** Stranger â†’ Partner â†’ Lover (precision-bound)  \n**N02 Arc:** Partner â†’ Co-leader â†’ Sacrificer  \n**N03â€“N05 Arc:** (To be detailed below)\n\n---\n\n## TIER 2: PRIMARY SUPPORTS\n\n\n"

          "text": "## EMOTIONAL INTENSITY MAP\n\n**Chapter Intensity Distribution:**\n- **High (4 chapters):** 3, 10, 11, 12\n- **Medium (6 chapters):** 4, 5, 6, 8, 9, 12\n- **Low (2 chapters):** 1, 2, 7\n\n**Arc Pattern:** Low opening → High crisis → Medium stabilization → High final crisis → Medium resolution\n\n---\n\n## SOULPULSE USAGE TRACKING\n\n**Total Uses in N01:** 7 confirmed uses\n- Ch 3: 2 uses (beam, building support)\n- Ch 4: 1 use (triage wall) + 1 failed attempt\n- Ch 6: 1 use (arch coordination)\n- Ch 8: 1 use (efficiency test)\n- Ch 10: 1 use (main support, near-Stage 2)\n\n**Stage Progression:**\n- Ch 1-2: Stage 0 (pre-Piercing)\n- Ch 3: Stage 0 → 1 onset\n- Ch 4-9: Stage 1 baseline\n- Ch 10: Approaching Stage 2 threshold (warning signs)\n- Ch 11-12: Stage 1 stabilized with management\n\n---\n\n## RELATIONSHIP PROGRESSION TRACKING\n\n**Arc Across N01:**\n- Ch 1-2: External awareness (reputation only)\n- Ch 3: First meeting (competence recognition)\n- Ch 4-5: Deliberate observation (measurement phase)\n- Ch 6: First coordination (partnership action)\n- Ch 7-8: Trust building (resistance → acceptance)\n- Ch 9: First explicit choice (each other over safety)\n- Ch 10: Conflict through care (control vs. autonomy)\n- Ch 11: Trauma bonding (vulnerability exchange)\n- Ch 12: Commitment formalized (shared principles)\n\n**By N01 End:** Load-bearing partnership established, functional love (not romantic confession)\n\n---\n\n## WORD COUNT BUDGET\n\n| Segment | Target | Status |\n|---------|--------|--------|\n| Ch 1 | 1,240 | 🔒 Locked |\n| Ch 2 | 1,280 | 🔒 Locked |\n| Ch 3 | 2,200 | 🔒 Locked |\n| Ch 4 | 2,350 | 🔒 Locked |\n| Ch 5 | 2,100 | 🔒 Locked |\n| Ch 6 | ~1,800 | To Write |\n| Ch 7 | ~1,500 | To Write |\n| I-1 | ~700 | To Write |\n| Ch 8 | ~1,600 | To Write |\n| Ch 9 | ~1,700 | To Write |\n| Ch 10 | ~1,800 | To Write |\n| I-2 | ~800 \n"
        },

    "64f69978-2583-4b58-8ce8-361f11f7f237": {
      "children": [
        "5bafbea1-e355-4515-a260-481030c21572"
      ],
      "id": "64f69978-2583-4b58-8ce8-361f11f7f237",
      "message": {
        "author": {
          "metadata": {},
          "name": "python",
          "role": "tool"
        },
        "channel": null,
        "content": {
          "content_type": "execution_output",
          "text": "(dict_keys(['POV', 'Tide', 'Setting', 'Beat Method', 'Summary', 'Stage Progression']),\n \"**Key Beats:**\\n- Opens with wrong hum detection (immediate sensory hook)\\n- Establishes Underface setting and Jhace's expertise\\n- Bram introduces caretaker/control theme\\n- First tremor (foreshadowing)\\n- Ends with Jhace at street level, sensing wrongness\")"
        },
        "create_time": 1765790061.8161998,
        "end_turn": null,
        "id": "64f69978-2583-4b58-8ce8-361f11f7f237",
        "metadata": {
          "aggregate_result": {
            "code": "# extract summary fields from each chunk: POV, Tide, Setting, Beat Method, Summary bullet etc.\ndef parse_chapter(chunk):\n    # chunk contains lines with \"- **POV:**\" etc\n    d={}\n    for field in [\"POV\",\"Tide\",\"Setting\",\"Beat Method\",\"Function\",\"Word Count Target\",\"Emotional Arc\",\"Soulpulse\"]:\n        m=re.search(rf\"- \\*\\*{re.escape(field)}:\\*\\* (.+)\", chunk)\n        if m: d[field]=m.group(1).strip()\n    # title? already\n    # capture \"Summary\" section: after \"**Summary:**\" until next blank line with \"**Key Elements\" etc\n    sm=re.search(r\"\\*\\*Summary:\\*\\*(.*?)(?:\\n\\*\\*Key Elements|\\n\\*\\*Soulpulse|\\n\\*\\*Stage|\\n\\*\\*Arc|\\Z)\", chunk, flags=re.S)\n    if sm:\n        d[\"Summary\"]=sm.group(1).strip()\n    # key elements bullets\n    km=re.search(r\"\\*\\*Key Elements:\\*\\*(.*?)(?:\\n\\*\\*Soulpulse|\\n\\*\\*Stage|\\n\\*\\*Arc|\\Z)\", chunk, flags=re.S)\n    if km:\n        d[\"Key Elements\"]=km.group(1).strip()\n    # stage progression\n    pm=re.search(r\"\\*\\*Stage Progression:\\*\\*(.*?)(?:\\n\\n---|\\Z)\", chunk, flags=re.S)\n    if pm: d[\"Stage Progression\"]=pm.group(1).strip()\n    return d\n\nchapter_data=[]\nfor num,title,chunk in chapters:\n    cd=parse_chapter(chunk)\n    chapter_data.append((int(num), title, cd))\nchapter_data[0][2].keys(), chapter_data[0][2].get(\"Summary\",\"\")[:300]\n",
            "end_time": 1765790061.9107864,
            "final_expression_output": "(dict_keys(['POV', 'Tide', 'Setting', 'Beat Method', 'Summary', 'Stage Progression']),\n \"**Key Beats:**\\n- Opens with wrong hum detection (immediate sensory hook)\\n- Establishes Underface setting and Jhace's expertise\\n- Bram introduces caretaker/control theme\\n- First tremor (foreshadowing)\\n- Ends with Jhace at street level, sensing wrongness\")",
            "in_kernel_exception": null,
            "jupyter_messages": [
              {
                "content": {
                  "execution_state": "busy"
                },
                "msg_type": "status",
                "parent_header": {
                  "msg_id": "3077b3ef-8ea9dd42307b327ffc80409f_3_1",
                  "version": "5.3"
                }
              },

                "msg_type": "execute_input",
                "parent_header": {
                  "msg_id": "3077b3ef-8ea9dd42307b327ffc80409f_3_1",
                  "version": "5.3"
                }

                "content": {
                  "data": {
                    "text/plain": "(dict_keys(['POV', 'Tide', 'Setting', 'Beat Method', 'Summary', 'Stage Progression']),\n \"**Key Beats:**\\n- Opens with wrong hum detection (immediate sensory hook)\\n- Establishes Underface setting and Jhace's expertise\\n- Bram introduces caretaker/control theme\\n- First tremor (foreshadowing)\\n- Ends with Jhace at street level, sensing wrongness\")"
                  }

    "67148f2b-c1ca-4f02-9e6e-f4a46732bbcc": {
      "children": [
        "c67d273e-89d0-41a8-b279-11c89d9c4b7a"
      ],
      "id": "67148f2b-c1ca-4f02-9e6e-f4a46732bbcc",
      "message": {
        "author": {
          "metadata": {},
          "name": "python",
          "role": "tool"
        },
        "channel": null,
        "content": {
          "content_type": "execution_output",
          "text": "### 3.2 Distance Units\n\nFrom smallest to largest (body to continent):\n\n- **Finger** (~2 cm)  \n- **Hand** (~10 cm)  \n- **Span** (~23 cm)  \n- **Forearm** (~45 cm)  \n- **Stride** (~90 cm)  \n- **Bar** (~1.8 m)  \n- **Rod** (~9 m)  \n- **Cable** (~45 m)  \n- **Vein-span** (~225 m)  \n- **Circuit** (~1.1 km)  \n- **Grid** (~5.5 km)  \n- **Reach** (~27.5 km)  \n- **Expanse** (~137 km)\n\n**Usage rules:**\n\n- Close detail: finger, hand, span (\"The gap was three fingers wide\").  \n- Room / workshop: stride, bar (\"The arch stood a bar and a half tall\").  \n- Street / block / infrastructure: rod, cable, vein-span.  \n- Neighborhood / district / city: circuit, grid.  \n- Regional / continental: reach, expanse.\n\nAvoid metric/imperial in narrative. If you need a \"translation,\" keep it in out-of-story material (bibles, notes), not in the prose.\n\n\n"
        },
        "create_time": 1765790210.0482395,
        "end_turn": null,
        "id": "67148f2b-c1ca-4f02-9e6e-f4a46732bbcc",
        "metadata": {
          "aggregate_result": {
            "code": "# extract distance units section 3.2 in lexicon\nm=re.search(r\"### 3\\.2 Distance Units\", lex)\nstart=m.start()\nm2=re.search(r\"### 3\\.3\", lex[m.end():])\nend=m.end()+m2.start() if m2 else len(lex)\nprint(lex[start:end])\n",
            "end_time": 1765790210.131744,
            "final_expression_output": null,
            "in_kernel_exception": null,
            "jupyter_messages": [
              {
                "content": {
                  "execution_state": "busy"
                },
                "msg_type": "status",
                "parent_header": {
                  "msg_id": "3077b3ef-8ea9dd42307b327ffc80409f_3_1",
                  "version": "5.3"
                }
              },
              {
                "msg_type": "execute_input",
                "parent_header": {
                  "msg_id": "3077b3ef-8ea9dd42307b327ffc80409f_3_1",
                  "version": "5.3"
                }
              },
              {
                "content": {
                  "name": "stdout",
                  "text": "### 3.2 Distance Units\n\nFrom smallest to largest (body to continent):\n\n- **Finger** (~2 cm)  \n- **Hand** (~10 cm)  \n- **Span** (~23 cm)  \n- **Forearm** (~45 cm)  \n- **Stride** (~90 cm)  \n- **Bar** (~1.8 m)  \n- **Rod** (~9 m)  \n- **Cable** (~45 m)  \n- **Vein-span** (~225 m)  \n- **Circuit** (~1.1 km)  \n- **Grid** (~5.5 km)  \n- **Reach** (~27.5 km)  \n- **Expanse** (~137 km)\n\n**Usage rules:**\n\n- Close detail: finger, hand, span (\"The gap was three fingers wide\").  \n- Room / workshop: stride, bar (\"The arch stood a bar and a half tall\").  \n- Street / block / infrastructure: rod, cable, vein-span.  \n- Neighborhood / district / city: circuit, grid.  \n- Regional / continental: reach, expanse.\n\nAvoid metric/imperial in narrative. If you need a \"translation,\" keep it in out-of-story material (bibles, notes), not in the prose.\n\n\n"
                },
                "msg_type": "stream",
                "parent_header": {
                  "msg_id": "3077b3ef-8ea9dd42307b327ffc80409f_3_1",
                  "version": "5.3"
                }
              },
              {
                "content": {
                  "execution_state": "idle"
                },
                "msg_type": "status",
                "parent_header": {
                  "msg_id": "3077b3ef-8ea9dd42307b327ffc80409f_3_1",
                  "version": "5.3"
                }
              }
            ],
            "messages": [
              {
                "message_type": "stream",
                "sender": "server",
                "stream_name": "stdout",
                "text": "### 3.2 Distance Units\n\nFrom smallest to largest (body to continent):\n\n- **Finger** (~2 cm)  \n- **Hand** (~10 cm)  \n- **Span** (~23 cm)  \n- **Forearm** (~45 cm)  \n- **Stride** (~90 cm)  \n- **Bar** (~1.8 m)  \n- **Rod** (~9 m)  \n- **Cable** (~45 m)  \n- **Vein-span** (~225 m)  \n- **Circuit** (~1.1 km)  \n- **Grid** (~5.5 km)  \n- **Reach** (~27.5 km)  \n- **Expanse** (~137 km)\n\n**Usage rules:**\n\n- Close detail: finger, hand, span (\"The gap was three fingers wide\").  \n- Room / workshop: stride, bar (\"The arch stood a bar and a half tall\").  \n- Street / block / infrastructure: rod, cable, vein-span.  \n- Neighborhood / district / city: circuit, grid.  \n- Regional / continental: reach, expanse.\n\nAvoid metric/imperial in narrative. If you need a \"translation,\" keep it in out-of-story material (bibles, notes), not in the prose.\n\n\n",
                "time": 1765790210.114623
              }
            ],
            "run_id": "db15cbfd-bb57-4aec-945d-325e0d667609",
            "start_time": 1765790210.0482395,
            "status": "success",
            "system_exception": null,
            "timeout_triggered": null,
            "update_time": 1765790210.131744
          },

          "classifier_response": "default",
          "default_model_slug": "gpt-5-2-thinking",

    "7021fed6-2318-45c9-a958-f85b7de12122": {
      "children": [
        "e8da9f19-e07c-4791-abf4-41ad61160636"
      ],
      "id": "7021fed6-2318-45c9-a958-f85b7de12122",
      "message": {
        "author": {
          "metadata": {},
          "name": null,
          "role": "assistant"
        },
        "channel": null,
        "content": {
          "content_type": "code",
          "language": "unknown",
          "response_format_name": null,
          "text": "def get_chapter_chunk(n):\n    for num,title,chunk in chapters:\n        if int(num)==n:\n            return title, chunk\nfor n in range(1,5):\n    title, chunk = get_chapter_chunk(n)\n    print(\"\\n====== CH\", n, title,\"======\")\n    print(chunk[:1800])\n"
        },
        "create_time": 1765790061.934024,
        "end_turn": false,
        "id": "7021fed6-2318-45c9-a958-f85b7de12122",
        "metadata": {
          "can_save": false,
          "citations": [],

            ],

          "parent_id": "5bafbea1-e355-4515-a260-481030c21572",

          "request_id": "cf485e74-b4b9-455d-acdd-084ed4857396",

    "733e5f05-f48f-4d40-9e94-e92885caf9ae": {
      "children": [
        "99e27666-63a8-4c96-bd31-18c11261e42d"
      ],
      "id": "733e5f05-f48f-4d40-9e94-e92885caf9ae",
      "message": {
        "author": {
          "metadata": {},
          "name": null,
          "role": "assistant"
        },
        "channel": null,
        "content": {
          "content_type": "code",
          "language": "unknown",
          "response_format_name": null,
          "text": "def extract_key_beats(chunk):\n    m = re.search(r\"\\*\\*Key Beats:\\*\\*(.*?)(?:\\n\\n\\*\\*Soulpulse:\\*\\*|\\n\\*\\*Soulpulse:\\*\\*|\\Z)\", chunk, flags=re.S)\n    return m.group(1).strip() if m else \"\"\ndef extract_soulpulse(chunk):\n    m = re.search(r\"\\*\\*Soulpulse:\\*\\*(.*?)(?:\\n\\n\\*\\*Stage Progression:\\*\\*|\\n\\*\\*Stage Progression:\\*\\*|\\Z)\", chunk, flags=re.S)\n    return m.group(1).strip() if m else \"\"\ndef extract_stage(chunk):\n    m = re.search(r\"\\*\\*Stage Progression:\\*\\*(.*?)(?:\\n\\n---|\\Z)\", chunk, flags=re.S)\n    return m.group(1).strip() if m else \"\"\nsummary_table=[]\nfor n in range(1,13):\n    title, chunk = get_chapter_chunk(n)\n    pov=re.search(r\"\\*\\*POV:\\*\\* (.+)\", chunk).group(1)\n    tide=re.search(r\"\\*\\*Tide:\\*\\* (.+)\", chunk).group(1)\n    setting=re.search(r\"\\*\\*Setting:\\*\\* (.+)\", chunk).group(1)\n    beat=re.search(r\"\\*\\*Beat Method:\\*\\* (.+)\", chunk).group(1)\n    emo=re.search(r\"\\*\\*Emotional Intensity:\\*\\* (.+)\", chunk).group(1)\n    keybeats=extract_key_beats(chunk)\n    soulpulse=extract_soulpulse(chunk)\n    stage=extract_stage(chunk)\n    summary_table.append([n,title,pov,tide,setting,beat,emo,soulpulse,stage,keybeats])\nsummary_table[3][0:4], summary_table[3][7]\n"
        },
        "create_time": 1765790084.024637,
        "end_turn": false,
        "id": "733e5f05-f48f-4d40-9e94-e92885caf9ae",
        "metadata": {
          "can_save": false,
          "citations": [],
          "classifier_response": "default",
          "content_references": [],
          "default_model_slug": "gpt-5-2-thinking",

              200012
            ],
            "type": "stop"

        "content": {
          "content_type": "execution_output",
          "text": "# SOULPULSE: MAGIC SYSTEM CORE\n**Resonance, Power Stages, Costs, and Limits**\n\n---\n\n## DOCUMENT PURPOSE\n\nThis document defines how Soulpulse actually works at meta-level, from first emergence after the Piercing through post-Fathom-Break escalation to late-saga combat resonance. [attached_file:9]  \nIt exists to keep power levels, costs, and capabilities consistent across all 30 novellas, even when characters only know fragments in-story. [attached_file:7]  \n\n**Status:** OMNISCIENT CANON (behind-the-scenes truth)  \n**Characters:** Never see this document; they discover partial truths only. [attached_file:9]  \n\nCross-refs:  \n- `00-Saga-Blueprint.md` â€” structure, Turnings, acrostic. [attached_file:6]  \n- `01-TeOga-Physical-Piercing.md` â€” why Soulpulse becomes possible. [attached_file:8]  \n- `03-Divine-Principles.md` â€” gods behind the Principles. [attached_file:12]  \n- `05-Knowledge-Gates-Discovery.md` â€” who learns what, when. [attached_file:6]  \n\n---\n\n## 1. ONTOLOGY: WHAT SOULPULSE IS\n\n### 1.1 Friction Engine of Existence\n\nAt cosmic level, existence is driven by constant contradiction between four Divine Principles: Form, Wholeness, Purity, and Entropy. [attached_file:12]  \nTheir mutual opposition generates a continuous â€œfrictionâ€ field, a kind of background soul-energy radiating through the universe. [attached_file:9]  \n\nOn Teâ€™Oga, this friction expresses as an omnipresent but previously inaccessible resonance field threaded through matter, bodies, and thought. [attached_file:9]  \nBefore the Piercing, this field exists but cannot be directly harnessed by mortals; it underlies life itself but is not â€œmagic.â€ [attached_file:10]  \n\n### 1.2 Piercing Interface\n\nThe Piercing does three critical things: [attached_file:8]  \n\n1. Scrapes **Soulpulse dust** from the projectile into the planetâ€™s interior and atmosphere. [attached_file:9]  \n2. Seeds this dust into water, soil, stone, and living bodies across the globe. [attached_file:8]  \n3. Changes Teâ€™Ogaâ€™s\n"
        },

    "7d7f6f3b-504b-46d6-ada0-9a257fcf31b2": {

        "6369cfcf-678e-411a-8770-d69dad322ce2"

      "message": {
        "author": {
          "metadata": {},

          "role": "tool"
        },
        "channel": null,
        "content": {
          "content_type": "execution_output",
          "text": "## 3. MEASUREMENT LEXICON USAGE\n\nAll in-world measurement uses the Te'Oga systems: time, distance, and mass.  \nMetric/imperial terms (meters, miles, kilograms, seconds, weeks) **never** appear in character-facing prose.\n\n### 3.1 Time Units\n\nCanonical units:\n\n- **Tide** â€“ day (~24 hours)  \n- **Anneal** â€“ 5 Tides (week)  \n- **Crucible** â€“ 7 Anneals (35 Tides; month)  \n- **Casting** â€“ 9 Crucibles (year)  \n- Higher (Smelting, Masterwork, Epoch) appear only in historical or mythic context.\n\nDaily periods within a Tide:\n\n- **Kindling** â€“ dawn / start of work  \n- **Pouring** â€“ mid-morning  \n- **Shaping** â€“ midday  \n- **Striking** â€“ afternoon  \n- **The Quench** â€“ dusk / rest  \n\n**Usage rules:**\n\n- Use Te'Oga terms directly:\n  - \"Two tides later,\" not \"two days later.\"  \n  - \"Within this anneal,\" not \"within this week.\"  \n  - \"In his thirtieth casting,\" not \"at thirty years old.\"  \n- One-time soft equivalences (\"a tideâ€”the length of a day\") may be used sparingly in early N01 to orient new readers; avoid afterward.\n\n### 3.2 Distance Units\n\nFrom smallest to largest (body to continent):\n\n- **Finger** (~2 cm)  \n- **Hand** (~10 cm)  \n- **Span** (~23 cm)  \n- **Forearm** (~45 cm)  \n- **Stride** (~90 cm)  \n- **Bar** (~1.8 m)  \n- **Rod** (~9 m)  \n- **Cable** (~45 m)  \n- **Vein-span** (~225 m)  \n- **Circuit** (~1.1 km)  \n- **Grid** (~5.5 km)  \n- **Reach** (~27.5 km)  \n- **Expanse** (~137 km)\n\n**Usage rules:**\n\n- Close detail: finger, hand, span (\"The gap was three fingers wide\").  \n- Room / workshop: stride, bar (\"The arch stood a bar and a half tall\").  \n- Street / block / infrastructure: rod, cable, vein-span.  \n- Neighborhood / district / city: circuit, grid.  \n- Regional / continental: reach, expanse.\n\nAvoid metric/imperial in narrative. If you need a \"translation,\" keep it in out-of-story material (bibles, notes), not in the prose.\n\n### 3.3 Mass Units\n\nKey units:\n\n- **Grain** â€“ smallest particles  \n- **Pinch** â€“ powders, spices, frit  \n- **Handful** â€“ small solids  \n- **Stone** â€“ personal lifting weight (~7.5 kg)  \n- **Load** â€“ cart burden  \n- **Ingot** â€“ major structural element mass  \n- **Pour** â€“ foundry batch  \n- **Vein-mass** â€“ infrastructure segment\n\n**Usage rules:**\n\n- Use small units for craft minutiae:\n  - \"Add a pinch of copper frit.\"  \n- Use mid units for tools/components:\n  - \"The bracket weighed a stone and a half.\"  \n- Use large units for infrastructure:\n  - \"The broken section massed nearly a full vein-mass.\"\n\n---\n\n\n"
        },
        "create_time": 1765789988.8919706,
        "end_turn": null,
        "id": "7d7f6f3b-504b-46d6-ada0-9a257fcf31b2",
        "metadata": {
          "aggregate_result": {
            "code": "# extract full measurement units section\nm = re.search(r\"## 3\\. MEASUREMENT LEXICON USAGE\", lex)\nstart=m.start()\nm2=re.search(r\"^## \", lex[m.end():], flags=re.M)\nend=m.end()+m2.start() if m2 else len(lex)\nprint(lex[start:end])\n",
            "end_time": 1765789988.9695532,
            "final_expression_output": null,
            "in_kernel_exception": null,
            "jupyter_messages": [

              {
                "content": {
                  "name": "stdout",
                  "text": "## 3. MEASUREMENT LEXICON USAGE\n\nAll in-world measurement uses the Te'Oga systems: time, distance, and mass.  \nMetric/imperial terms (meters, miles, kilograms, seconds, weeks) **never** appear in character-facing prose.\n\n### 3.1 Time Units\n\nCanonical units:\n\n- **Tide** â€“ day (~24 hours)  \n- **Anneal** â€“ 5 Tides (week)  \n- **Crucible** â€“ 7 Anneals (35 Tides; month)  \n- **Casting** â€“ 9 Crucibles (year)  \n- Higher (Smelting, Masterwork, Epoch) appear only in historical or mythic context.\n\nDaily periods within a Tide:\n\n- **Kindling** â€“ dawn / start of work  \n- **Pouring** â€“ mid-morning  \n- **Shaping** â€“ midday  \n- **Striking** â€“ afternoon  \n- **The Quench** â€“ dusk / rest  \n\n**Usage rules:**\n\n- Use Te'Oga terms directly:\n  - \"Two tides later,\" not \"two days later.\"  \n  - \"Within this anneal,\" not \"within this week.\"  \n  - \"In his thirtieth casting,\" not \"at thirty years old.\"  \n- One-time soft equivalences (\"a tideâ€”the length of a day\") may be used sparingly in early N01 to orient new readers; avoid afterward.\n\n### 3.2 Distance Units\n\nFrom smallest to largest (body to continent):\n\n- **Finger** (~2 cm)  \n- **Hand** (~10 cm)  \n- **Span** (~23 cm)  \n- **Forearm** (~45 cm)  \n- **Stride** (~90 cm)  \n- **Bar** (~1.8 m)  \n- **Rod** (~9 m)  \n- **Cable** (~45 m)  \n- **Vein-span** (~225 m)  \n- **Circuit** (~1.1 km)  \n- **Grid** (~5.5 km)  \n- **Reach** (~27.5 km)  \n- **Expanse** (~137 km)\n\n**Usage rules:**\n\n- Close detail: finger, hand, span (\"The gap was three fingers wide\").  \n- Room / workshop: stride, bar (\"The arch stood a bar and a half tall\").  \n- Street / block / infrastructure: rod, cable, vein-span.  \n- Neighborhood / district / city: circuit, grid.  \n- Regional / continental: reach, expanse.\n\nAvoid metric/imperial in narrative. If you need a \"translation,\" keep it in out-of-story material (bibles, notes), not in the prose.\n\n### 3.3 Mass Units\n\nKey units:\n\n- **Grain** â€“ smallest particles  \n- **Pinch** â€“ powders, spices, frit  \n- **Handful** â€“ small solids  \n- **Stone** â€“ personal lifting weight (~7.5 kg)  \n- **Load** â€“ cart burden  \n- **Ingot** â€“ major structural element mass  \n- **Pour** â€“ foundry batch  \n- **Vein-mass** â€“ infrastructure segment\n\n**Usage rules:**\n\n- Use small units for craft minutiae:\n  - \"Add a pinch of copper frit.\"  \n- Use mid units for tools/components:\n  - \"The bracket weighed a stone and a half.\"  \n- Use large units for infrastructure:\n  - \"The broken section massed nearly a full vein-mass.\"\n\n---\n\n\n"
                },

    "80234933-0823-45c9-8d9d-63ec3957c525": {
      "children": [
        "efd14ea7-6793-4ede-bbb2-72a969cdcb3b"
      ],
      "id": "80234933-0823-45c9-8d9d-63ec3957c525",
      "message": {
        "author": {
          "metadata": {},
          "name": "python",
          "role": "tool"
        },
        "channel": null,
        "content": {
          "content_type": "execution_output",
          "text": "[(1,\n  'Something Wrong',\n  'Jhace (tight third)',\n  'Underface beneath Saint Merro, heat-grid inspection run'),\n (2, 'Glass That Listens', '', \"Observatory platform at Saint Merro's rim\"),\n (3,\n  'The Sky Breaks',\n  'Jhace (tight third)',\n  'Underface tunnel, then street level at building support'),\n (4,\n  'After the Shudder',\n  'Jhace (tight third)',\n  'Triage chamber, Underface tunnels, kiln street')]"
        },
        "create_time": 1765790154.095366,
        "end_turn": null,
        "id": "80234933-0823-45c9-8d9d-63ec3957c525",
        "metadata": {
          "aggregate_result": {
            "code": "def parse_segment(seg_text):\n    # title line first\n    first_line = seg_text.splitlines()[0]\n    # Extract number/title\n    if \"CHAPTER\" in first_line:\n        m=re.search(r'CHAPTER (\\d+): \"([^\"]+)\"', first_line)\n        num=int(m.group(1)) if m else None\n        title=m.group(2) if m else None\n        kind=\"CHAPTER\"\n    elif \"INTERLUDE\" in first_line:\n        m=re.search(r'INTERLUDE ([IVX]+): \"([^\"]+)\"', first_line)\n        num=m.group(1) if m else None\n        title=m.group(2) if m else None\n        kind=\"INTERLUDE\"\n    else:\n        kind=\"OTHER\"; num=None; title=None\n    # fields\n    fields={}\n    for field in [\"POV\",\"Tide\",\"Setting\",\"Beat Method\",\"Saga Beat\",\"Type\",\"Word Count\",\"Word Count Target\",\"Target Word Count\",\"Status\",\"Emotional Intensity\",\"Function\",\"Arc Pattern\",\"Purpose\"]:\n        fields[field]=get_field(seg_text, field)\n    keybeats=extract_key_beats(seg_text)\n    soulpulse=extract_soulpulse(seg_text)\n    stage=extract_stage(seg_text)\n    summary= re.search(r\"\\*\\*Summary:\\*\\*(.*?)(?:\\n\\n\\*\\*Key Elements:|\\n\\*\\*Key Beats:|\\n\\*\\*Purpose:|\\Z)\", seg_text, flags=re.S)\n    # But summary block sometimes empty, key beats etc\n    return dict(kind=kind,num=num,title=title,fields=fields,keybeats=keybeats,soulpulse=soulpulse,stage=stage,text=seg_text)\n\nparsed=[]\nfor label, seg_text in segments:\n    parsed.append(parse_segment(seg_text))\n# check chapter2 parsed fields\n[(p['num'],p['title'],p['fields']['POV'],p['fields']['Setting']) for p in parsed if p['kind']==\"CHAPTER\"][:4]\n",
            "end_time": 1765790154.2135572,
            "final_expression_output": "[(1,\n  'Something Wrong',\n  'Jhace (tight third)',\n  'Underface beneath Saint Merro, heat-grid inspection run'),\n (2, 'Glass That Listens', '', \"Observatory platform at Saint Merro's rim\"),\n (3,\n  'The Sky Breaks',\n  'Jhace (tight third)',\n  'Underface tunnel, then street level at building support'),\n (4,\n  'After the Shudder',\n  'Jhace (tight third)',\n  'Triage chamber, Underface tunnels, kiln street')]",
            "in_kernel_exception": null,
            "jupyter_messages": [
              {
                "content": {
                  "execution_state": "busy"
                },

          "text": "## FORBIDDEN SEQUENCES (N01 CREATES)\n\n**Two-Chapter Sequences That Cannot Repeat in Future Novellas:**\n1. BEAT_01 → BEAT_01 (Ch 1→2)\n2. BEAT_01 → BEAT_03 (Ch 2→3)\n3.     "852e1fc7-cb6d-4547-ab87-724759d7535a": {
      "children": [
        "80234933-0823-45c9-8d9d-63ec3957c525"
      ],
      "id": "852e1fc7-cb6d-4547-ab87-724759d7535a",
      "message": {
        "author": {
          "metadata": {},
          "name": null,
          "role": "assistant"
        },
        "channel": null,
        "content": {
          "content_type": "code",
          "language": "unknown",
          "response_format_name": null,
          "text": "def parse_segment(seg_text):\n    # title line first\n    first_line = seg_text.splitlines()[0]\n    # Extract number/title\n    if \"CHAPTER\" in first_line:\n        m=re.search(r'CHAPTER (\\d+): \"([^\"]+)\"', first_line)\n        num=int(m.group(1)) if m else None\n        title=m.group(2) if m else None\n        kind=\"CHAPTER\"\n    elif \"INTERLUDE\" in first_line:\n        m=re.search(r'INTERLUDE ([IVX]+): \"([^\"]+)\"', first_line)\n        num=m.group(1) if m else None\n        title=m.group(2) if m else None\n        kind=\"INTERLUDE\"\n    else:\n        kind=\"OTHER\"; num=None; title=None\n    # fields\n    fields={}\n    for field in [\"POV\",\"Tide\",\"Setting\",\"Beat Method\",\"Saga Beat\",\"Type\",\"Word Count\",\"Word Count Target\",\"Target Word Count\",\"Status\",\"Emotional Intensity\",\"Function\",\"Arc Pattern\",\"Purpose\"]:\n        fields[field]=get_field(seg_text, field)\n    keybeats=extract_key_beats(seg_text)\n    soulpulse=extract_soulpulse(seg_text)\n    stage=extract_stage(seg_text)\n    summary= re.search(r\"\\*\\*Summary:\\*\\*(.*?)(?:\\n\\n\\*\\*Key Elements:|\\n\\*\\*Key Beats:|\\n\\*\\*Purpose:|\\Z)\", seg_text, flags=re.S)\n    # But summary block sometimes empty, key beats etc\n    return dict(kind=kind,num=num,title=title,fields=fields,keybeats=keybeats,soulpulse=soulpulse,stage=stage,text=seg_text)\n\nparsed=[]\nfor label, seg_text in segments:\n    parsed.append(parse_segment(seg_text))\n# check chapter2 parsed fields\n[(p['num'],p['title'],p['fields']['POV'],p['fields']['Setting']) for p in parsed if p['kind']==\"CHAPTER\"][:4]\n"
        },
        "create_time": 1765790143.765477,
        "end_turn": false,
        "id": "852e1fc7-cb6d-4547-ab87-724759d7535a",
        "metadata": {
          "can_save": false,
          "citations": [],
          "classifier_response": "default",

          "default_model_slug": "gpt-5-2-thinking",

        "channel": null,
        "content": {
          "content_type": "execution_output",
          "text": "## 2. TURNING 1: GLASS (N01â€“N05)\n\n### Knowledge State at N01 Start\n\n**Jhace:**\n- Craft-mind: instinctively feels Soulpulse in materials\n- Knows nothing about divine origins or cosmic truth\n- Discovers cost of use through trial and pain\n- Believes Soulpulse is \"new magic from the Piercing debris\"\n\n- Craft-mind: instinctively feels Soulpulse in glass and structure\n- Same blank slate as Jhace regarding bigger picture\n- By N04: realizes this power has principles/rules, not random\n- By N05: pushes into knowledge about cost-and-consequence through desperate use\n\n**Corlexi (masked Xilcore):**\n- Experiences \"vague alignment\" to Form without naming it\n- Sees practical Soulpulse as compliance and liability issue\n- Has vague dreams/memories he dismisses as stress\n- No conscious divine identity yet\n\n**Mobel (masked Blemo):**\n- Gets shard in leg from exit-event projectile chunk\n- Experiences hyper-clarity about tide and pressure\n- Assumes it's normal harbor-sense magnified, not Soulpulse\n- Interludes show him starting to remember old things (hidden from main POV)\n\n**Salee (masked Leesa):**\n- Finds herself unusually able to mend injuries and restore trust\n- No framework for understanding why\n- Chalks it up to luck and good instincts\n- Interludes show subtle memory flashes\n\n**Eries (masked Seeri):**\n- Watches systems fail and accelerate decay; finds it beautiful\n- No conscious awareness this is Soulpulse-adjacent\n- Philosophy stays unchanged; tools deepen\n- Interludes show her slowly realizing structures are \"listening\" to her will\n\n**Church/Guild/Authority:**\n- Confused and reactive\n- Theorize it's contamination, possession, or heresy\n- Begin registration/suppression attempts (fail)\n- No framework for understanding yet\n\n### What Gets Revealed in T1\n\n**On-Page (Characters Learn):**\n- N01: Soulpulse can stabilize and break structures\n- N02: Some people are naturally aligned to it; others struggle or can't access\n- N03: It has costs; use causes fatigue, tremors, pain\n- N04: Cost escalates with intensity; there are hard limits\n- N05: One person can hold multiple Principles briefly (Jhace's Fathom Break is proof)\n\n- The Piercing was a designed weapon, not random impact\n- Corlexi/Mobel/Salee/Eries are masked gods\n- Gods are beginning to awaken via Fathom Break pulse\n- Soulpulse is friction-field access, not new creation\n\n### Post-Fathom Break Knowledge Shock (N06)\n\nJhace's Break does three things to knowledge:\n1. Proves a human can temporarily hold all Principles\n2. Lowers barrier so Tier 3 mortals manifest involuntarily\n3. Wakes gods to partial memory (readers see in interludes, main characters don't yet)\n\n---\n\n\n"
        },
        "create_time": 1765790011.26885,
        "end_turn": null,
        "id": "8b3afb2d-afda-4845-86fb-25f720d6a970",
        "metadata": {

          "text": "# OMNIMETA SAGA - CUSTOM GPT PACKAGE\n\nThis package contains everything needed to create your OmniMeta Saga Architect Custom GPT.\n\n## QUICK START\n\n1. Open **CUSTOM_GPT_SETUP.md**\n2. Follow the setup steps\n3. Upload the included files to your Custom GPT\n\n## FILES INCLUDED\n\n### Setup Guide\n- **CUSTOM_GPT_SETUP.md** - Complete configuration guide\n\n### Canonical Files (Upload these to Custom GPT)\n- 00-Saga-Blueprint.md\n- Story_Bible_Template_Structure_v2_1.md\n- OMNIMETA_SAGA_META_CANON_MEMORY.md\n- 01-TeOga-Physical-Piercing.md\n- 02-Soulpulse-Core.md\n- 03-Divine-Principles.md\n- 04-Cosmology-Confluence.md\n- 05-Knowledge-Gates-Discovery.md\n- 10-World-TeOga.md\n- 11-Factions-Politics.md\n- 30-Turning1-All-Characters.md\n- 50-Lexicon-Style.md\n\n### Reference Files\n- N01_Chapter_Grid_12ch_REVISED.md (example implementation)\n- N01_Beat_Mapping_Layer_FINAL.md (beat mapping system)\n\n## STRUCTURE\n\n### Novella Format (Universal)\n- 12 numbered chapters (1,200-2,200 words each)\n- 2 interludes (600-900 words each)\n- 1 epilogue (800-1,000 words)\n- = 15 total narrative segments\n- Total: ~18,000-21,000 words per novella\n\n### Acrostic\nGLASS-CRACK-FORGE-SMELT-CLEAR-BREAK (6 Turnings × 5 novellas each)\n\n### Key Systems\n- Beat Unpredictability (no pattern repetition)\n- Lexicon Enforcement (Te'Oga vocabulary only)\n- Cost Visibility (Soulpulse extracts physical payment)\n- Knowledge Gates (innocent/guilty frame)\n\n## SETUP TIME\n\n~15-30 minutes to create Custom GPT\n\nOnce created, your GPT will generate production-ready story bibles and prose for all 30 novellas.\n\n"
        },
