          "response_format_name": null,
          "text": "bible = \"\"\"\n# N01 Story Bible — The Grip That Holds\n\n## Version History\n- v1.1-VERIFIED: Tide-stamp: production build — Passed all checkpoints\n- v2.0-COMPLETE: Tide-stamp: production build — Approved for prose generation\n- v2.1-LOCKED: Tide-stamp: production build — Canon-locked for Turning 1 continuity\n\n### Acrostic Verification Checkpoint ✓\n- [x] Working title key-letter matches Turning 1 acrostic position: **G** in **GLASS** (Position 1/5)\n- [x] Turning metaphor correctly applied: **GLASS** (fragile emergence; transparent bonds that can shatter)\n- [x] Novella numbered correctly: **N01** (saga opener)\n\n---\n\n## SECTION 0: NOVELLA PARAMETERS\n\n- **Novella ID:** N01\n- **Working Title:** *The Grip That Holds* (key-letter **G**)\n- **Turning:** 1 — **GLASS** (fragile formation; first bonds; breakable transparency)\n- **Acrostic Letter:** **G** in GLASS\n- **Acrostic Position:** 1/5 in Turning 1\n- **Total Segment Target:** ~20k words (12 chapters + 2 interludes + 1 epilogue)\n- **Chapter Word Count Target:** 1,200–2,200 with one sanctioned grid-outlier (Ch 4 target 2,350; locked)  \n  *(Source: `N01_Chapter_Grid_12ch_REVISED.md` — locked segment targets)*\n- **Interludes:** 2 (god‑vessel POV, innocent frame)\n- **Epilogue:** 1 (non-god POV; hook into N02 institutional pressure)\n\n---\n\n## SECTION 1: SAGA STRUCTURE CONTEXT\n\n### Fixed Saga Laws (applied here)\n- **30 novellas / 6 Turnings / 5 novellas per Turning** (acrostic: GLASS–CRACK–FORGE–SMELT–CLEAR–BREAK).  \n  *(Source: `OMNIMETA_SAGA_META_CANON_MEMORY.md`)*\n- **Dual-frame doctrine:** characters operate in the **innocent frame**; deep cosmology remains gated.  \n  *(Source: `05-Knowledge-Gates-Discovery.md`)*\n- **Soulpulse cost-visibility:** every use pays in body and function; costs accumulate and do not “reset.”  \n  *(Source: `02-Soulpulse-Core.md`)*\n\n### Variable Elements (N01 specific)\n- **Timeline anchor:** Tide 0–20 (Piercing through first stabilization wave).  \n- **Primary location:** Saint Merro district + Underface (Te’Oga’s Crown).  \n  *(Source: `10-World-TeOga.md`)*\n- **Turning 1 thematic spine:** “Hold what can be held—without letting holding become control.”\n\n---\n\n## SECTION 2: PRIOR NOVELLAS SUMMARY\n\n- **None.** N01 is the saga’s entry point.  \n- **Baseline carry-in (universal knowledge at Tide 0):**\n  - The world is tense, doctrine is contradictory, infrastructure is layered and old.\n  - The Piercing is imminent but not expected by mortals; no one yet understands Soulpulse.  \n  *(Source: `05-Knowledge-Gates-Discovery.md`, `01-TeOga-Physical-Piercing.md`)*\n\n---\n\n## SECTION 3: KNOWLEDGE-GATE COMPLIANCE\n\n### What the reader/characters may know in N01\n- The Piercing happened; exit-side quake damage is catastrophic.\n- Some people can “grip” matter in crisis (Soulpulse), but it behaves conditionally and hurts.\n- Doctrine argues: punishment vs test vs accident; none are confirmed.\n- Gods are **not** named as gods in character-facing narration; interludes maintain innocent frame.\n\n### What must remain concealed (do not state as fact in-prose)\n- Any explicit “designed weapon” certainty.\n- Any explicit identification of Corlexi/Mobel as gods.\n- Any mechanics beyond observed conditional activation, visible cost, and crude pattern inference.  \n*(Source: `05-Knowledge-Gates-Discovery.md`)*\n\n---\n\n## SECTION 4: CHAPTER BEAT PATTERN\n\n### Local Beat Plan (N01)\n*(Local beats: `N01_Beat_Mapping_Layer_FINAL.md`. Chapter placement: `N01_Chapter_Grid_12ch_REVISED.md`.)*\n\n| Segment | Local Beat | Saga Beat Mapping | Notes |\n|---|---|---:|---|\n| Ch 1 | BEAT_01 — Observation → Pattern Recognition | Beat 10 | Baseline craft-sense; pre-Piercing |\n| Ch 2 | BEAT_01 — Observation → Pattern Recognition | Beat 10 | Parallel baseline; pre-Piercing |\n| Ch 3 | BEAT_03 — Crisis Front-Loaded → Rolling Complications → Partial Resolution | Beat 3 | Piercing catastrophe; first use |\n| Ch 4 | BEAT_02 — Trial → Failure → Compounding → Success/Cost | Beat 1 | Conditional activation revealed |\n| Ch 5 | BEAT_01 — Observation → Pattern Recognition | Beat 10 | Mapping, refusal of co-option |\n| Ch 6 | BEAT_03 — Crisis Front-Loaded → Sustained Pressure → Narrow Success | Beat 3 | First true coordination |\n| Ch 7 | BEAT_08 — Recovery/Reflection → Recommitment | Beat 21 (variant) | Quiet calculation; approaching threshold |\n| I-1 | Interlude (Form vessel) | — | Divine pressure; innocent frame |\n| Ch 8 | BEAT_04 — Recalibration → Offer → Refusal/Choice | Beat 16 | Proof test; pride vs partnership |\n| Ch 9 | BEAT_07 — Political Pressure → Moral Line in Sand | Beat 26 | Institutional offer refused |\n| Ch 10 | BEAT_03 — External Threat → Narrow Escape | Beat 3 | Near-Stage 2 warning |\n| I-2 | Interlude (Purity vessel) | — | Second force; ideological sharpen |\n| Ch 11 | BEAT_08 — Recovery/Intimacy → Commitment | Beat 17 (variant) | Trauma exchange; bond locks |\n| Ch 12 | BEAT_08 — Reflection → Shared Purpose | Beat 21 (variant) | Three priorities; public vow |\n| Epi | Epilogue hook | — | N02 pressure line introduced |\n\n### Beat Unpredictability Locks Created by N01\n**Two-segment sequences forbidden to repeat exactly in later novellas:**  \n1) BEAT_01 → BEAT_01 (Ch 1→2)  \n2) BEAT_01 → BEAT_03 (Ch 2→3)  \n3) BEAT_03 → BEAT_02 (Ch 3→4)  \n4) BEAT_02 → BEAT_04 (Ch 4→5)  \n5) BEAT_04 → BEAT_03 (Ch 5→6)  \n\n**Three-segment sequences forbidden to repeat exactly:**  \n1) BEAT_01 → BEAT_01 → BEAT_03 (Ch 1→2→3)  \n2) BEAT_03 → BEAT_02 → BEAT_04 (Ch 3→4→5)  \n*(Source: `N01_Chapter_Grid_12ch_REVISED.md` — Forbidden Sequences)*\n\n---\n\n## SECTION 5: INTERLUDE PLACEMENT\n\n- **Interlude I (after Ch 7):** Corlexi — Form vessel POV; “System vs Chaos.”  \n  Purpose: show a Principle‑biased mind watching mortal stabilization efforts; foreshadow bureaucratic tightening without naming divinity.  \n- **Interlude II (after Ch 10):** Mobel — Purity vessel POV; “Purity Rising.”  \n  Purpose: introduce a second divine pressure vector and the sensation of purification-by-pain; innocent frame maintained.  \n*(Source: `N01_Chapter_Grid_12ch_REVISED.md`)*\n\n---\n\n## SECTION 6: CHAPTER SUMMARIES AND BEAT BREAKDOWN\n\n### ACT I — THE CUT (Ch 1–4)\n\n**Ch 1 — “Something Wrong”** (POV: Jhace; Tide 0, Kindling; Underface heat-grid)  \n- Beat: BEAT_01. Establish Jhace as Underface craft-mind; wrong hum, wrong stress, wrong stillness.  \n- Plot: inspection run; caretaker friend Bram presses him to come up; tremor foreshadowing.  \n- Soulpulse: none (Stage 0).  \n- End-state shift: unease becomes duty.\n\n**Ch 2 — “Glass That Listens”** (POV: Pella; Tide 0, Pouring; rim observatory over Saint Merro)  \n- Beat: BEAT_01 (parallel to Ch 1).  \n- Plot: Pella records distortions and stress through glass; introduces her care-through-measurement pattern; Marisse grief implied.  \n- Soulpulse: none (Stage 0).  \n- End-state shift: the sky “splits” into imminent rupture.\n\n**Ch 3 — “The Sky Breaks”** (POV: Jhace; Tide 0, Striking; Underface → street support)  \n- Beat: BEAT_03. Opens mid-catastrophe. Chloen trapped; Jhace’s “23 names” trauma spark.  \n- Soulpulse: **2 uses** (beam hold; building support). Requirements visible: skin contact + urgent protective need.  \n- Cost: heat in palms, tremor onset, vein‑lines and exhaustion after; collapse risk seeded.  \n- Relationship seed: Jhace meets Pella in competence-space (she measures; he holds).  \n- End-state shift: Stage 1 begins; the city becomes a living load.\n\n**Ch 4 — “After the Shudder”** (POV: Jhace; Tide 1, Kindling; triage chamber + kiln street)  \n- Beat: BEAT_02. Trial/failure teaches conditional activation.  \n- Soulpulse: **1 success + 1 failed attempt** (non-critical crack fails; triage wall succeeds).  \n- Cost: tremor worsens; fine motor loss (can’t grip nail).  \n- Relationship tension: Jhace bristles under oversight; Tellis (water-guild) logic forces compliance in triage.  \n- End-state shift: “the wall held; his grip did not.”\n\n### ACT II — THE PATTERN (Ch 5–9)\n\n**Ch 5 — “Lines That Don’t Lie”** (POV: Pella; Tide 2–3; kiln-corridor streets, hold sites)  \n- Beat: BEAT_01. Pattern mapping as survival.  \n- Plot: Pella documents activation conditions and cost progression; refuses early institutional co-option; refuses guild profit retreat.  \n- Relationship movement: practical care without coddling; watches Jhace decline; chooses to engage before he dies.  \n- Soulpulse: none.  \n- End-state shift: partnership becomes a decision, not an accident.\n\n**Ch 6 — “Weight That Won’t Yield”** (POV: Jhace; Tide 3–4; junction arch failure)  \n- Beat: BEAT_03. Crisis with people trapped.  \n- Soulpulse: **1 coordinated use** (Pella marks geometry; Jhace provides power).  \n- Cost: tremor persists; vein‑lines linger; cost reduction possible but not free.  \n- Relationship movement: first true partnership choreography.  \n- End-state shift: competence becomes trust.\n\n**Ch 7 — “Quiet Calculations”** (POV: Pella; Tide 5–6; logbook workroom + street overlook)  \n- Beat: BEAT_08 (reflection variant).  \n- Plot: data compilation; discovery of an exponential cost curve; realization Jhace is on a crash path toward a threshold.  \n- Soulpulse: none.  \n- End-state shift: she commits to confronting him with evidence.\n\n**Interlude I — “Corlexi: System vs Chaos”** (POV: Corlexi; Tide ~6–7; Crown administrative chambers)  \n- Innocent-frame divine lens: Form bias; sensory overwhelm managed by categorization.  \n- Purpose beats: institutional tightening; a “problem” identified (two mortals stabilizing without permission).  \n- No explicit god-language; no explicit cosmic truth.\n\n**Ch 8 — “Proof and Resistance”** (POV: Jhace; Tide 7–8; forge → joint holds)  \n- Beat: BEAT_04. Offer and refusal turns into choice.  \n- Plot: Pella proposes an efficiency method; Jhace resists (autonomy threatened); controlled test proves reduction in cost.  \n- Soulpulse: **1 controlled test**; cost reduced via targeting.  \n- End-state shift: he accepts partnership improves him.\n\n**Ch 9 — “Ash and Claims”** (POV: Pella; Tide 10–12; refugee camp + Crown contact)  \n- Beat: BEAT_07. Political pressure sharpens.  \n- Plot: Corvin and Tellis (guild/state) offer resources tied to control; Pella consults Jhace; public refusal chooses each other over safety.  \n- Soulpulse: none.  \n- End-state shift: enemies made; bond publicly declared by action.\n\n### ACT III — THE GRIP (Ch 10–12)\n\n**Ch 10 — “Near Collapse”** (POV: both alternating; Tide 14–15; main support crisis)  \n- Beat: BEAT_03. Hundreds at risk; perfect coordination; Jhace overextends under caretaker compulsion.  \n- Soulpulse: **1 major use**; near-threshold warning signs (brief sensory overload; vein‑lines visible at rest).  \n- Cost: Stage 2 approach without crossing; collapse risk; shaking, numbness, vision shimmer.  \n- Relationship stress: her control saves him; he hates needing it.  \n- End-state shift: need becomes undeniable.\n\n**Interlude II — “Mobel: Purity Rising”** (POV: Mobel; Tide ~15–16; Entry-side harbor city)  \n- Innocent-frame divine lens: pain as cleansing; judgment as instinct; first awareness of “other” forces without naming them.  \n- Purpose: foreshadow inquisitorial expansion; ideological warfare seed.\n\n**Ch 11 — “Holding Together”** (POV: both intimate; Tide 17–18)  \n- Beat: BEAT_08 (intimacy variant).  \n- Plot: post-crisis defenses down; trauma exchange (Jhace’s 23 names; Pella’s Marisse); operational love acknowledged; physical intimacy begins.  \n- Soulpulse: none; recovery and aftershocks visible.  \n- End-state shift: partnership becomes bond.\n\n**Ch 12 — “Choosing the Weight”** (POV: both balanced; Tide 19–20; rebuilt overlook + forge wall)  \n- Beat: BEAT_08 (purpose variant).  \n- Plot: walk rebuilt district; accept larger forces gathering; choose what they can hold.  \n- Three priorities written on the forge wall (public covenant):  \n  1) Hold lives before stone.  \n  2) Keep the work local and witnessed.  \n  3) Refuse ownership—by guild, church, or self.  \n- Soulpulse: none; tremor through linked hands as quiet reminder of cost.  \n- End-state shift: foundation locked; N02 pressure is inevitable.\n\n### Epilogue (Segment 15) — “The First Line”\n- POV: Bram (or a neutral Crown clerk), non-god. Tide 20+ (immediately after Ch 12).  \n- Content: a sealed notice arrives—registry language, licensing language, “safety” language—aimed at Jhace and Pella.  \n- Purpose: pivot from survival to institutional conflict; title-hook into N02 (*Lines Under Pressure*).\n\n---\n\n## SECTION 7: CHARACTER STATE TABLE\n\n| Character | Tier | Alignment Lean | Start State (Tide 0) | End State (Tide 20+) | Soulpulse State / Cost Visibility |\n|---|---:|---|---|---|---|\n| **Jhace Torrin** | 1 | Form-leaning by craft; Wholeness impulse by care | Underface forgemind; solitary stabilizer; unresolved “23 names” guilt | Public stabilizer; bonded to Pella; refuses institutional ownership | Stage 0→1 onset (Ch 3). Persistent tremor, vein‑lines under skin, fine motor loss after heavy use; near-threshold warning at Ch 10 |\n| **Pella** | 1 | Form-leaning via measurement; Purity temptation via self-judgment | Observer at rim; grief anchored to Marisse; care expressed as data | Partner/lover; co-lead stabilizations; makes public refusal with Jhace | Not primary “power source” in N01; enables targeting and cost reduction; shows secondary strain (headache, shaking after proximity work) |\n| **Bram Kethis** | 2 | Wholeness | Caretaker friend; pushes Jhace toward rest | Witness to bond; becomes their practical shield | No Soulpulse use; carries physical labor load and social heat |\n| **Chloen** | 2 | Neutral | Young apprentice; vulnerable during Piercing | Survives; healing side-effect observed; becomes symbol of “why hold” | No deliberate use; potential latent manifestation implied only |\n| **Corvin** | 2/3 | Form (institution) | Guild/state factor watching instability | Begins pressure campaign; offers tied to control | No Soulpulse shown; political leverage increases |\n| **Tellis (Water-Guild rep)** | 2/3 | Form with Purity rhetoric | Triage logic voice; crisis organizer | Aligns with Corvin in offer; becomes recurring institutional face | No Soulpulse shown in N01 |\n| **Ressa** | 3 | Wholeness | Seen in triage networks | Stabilizes the community weave; future manifestation seeded but not shown as fact | No overt use in N01 |\n| **Black-market trainer (nameless)** | 4 | Entropy-adjacent | Off-screen; rumor | Briefly encountered (Ch 6 context); offers technique at moral cost | No on-page use required; exists to set training loop pressure |\n| **Corlexi (Form vessel)** | Interlude | Form | Sensory overwhelm; categorizes to survive | Identifies “anomaly” (two mortals stabilizing); pushes system response | No overt divinity; perception is the tell |\n| **Mobel (Purity vessel)** | Interlude | Purity | Pain as cleansing; judgment reflex | Feels other forces; sets inquisitorial trajectory | No overt divinity; perception is the tell |\n\n---\n\n## SECTION 8: POLITICAL AND FACTION CONTEXT\n\n### Saint Merro / Crown Power Map (Turning 1)\n- **Guild Compact (local):** infrastructure control; wants stabilization routed through guild authority and profit channels.  \n- **Crown Council (civic):** wants order, registry, and predictable repairs; “safety” becomes a tool.  \n- **Church variants:** competing doctrines; Purity rhetoric rising on the Entry-side; Exit-side more fractured.  \n- **Underface workers/refugees:** demand immediate holding, not debates.\n\n### Immediate N01 Pressure Vector\n- Early registry language and “licensed stabilization” proposals begin circulating (proto-RRO energy without formal office).  \n- Corvin/Tellis represent the “help with strings” offer; refusal turns them into antagonists.  \n*(Source: `11-Factions-Politics.md`, adapted to pre-formalization state in Turning 1.)*\n\n---\n\n## SECTION 9: MAGIC / COST / PRINCIPLE CANON\n\n### Soulpulse in Turning 1 (grounded rules)\n- **No spectacle escalation:** effects stay materially grounded—stress lines, heat, subtle glow under strain.  \n- **Activation is conditional:** immediate protective need + contact triggers; casual tests often fail.  \n- **Range is intimate:** hands-on, structural touch, short pushes; no distant casting.  \n*(Source: `02-Soulpulse-Core.md`, Turning 1 constraints)*\n\n### N01 Usage Ledger (must match prose)\n- **Total uses:** 7 confirmed.  \n  - Ch 3: 2 uses (beam hold; building support)  \n  - Ch 4: 1 use (triage wall) + 1 failed attempt  \n  - Ch 6: 1 use (arch coordination)  \n  - Ch 8: 1 use (controlled efficiency test)  \n  - Ch 10: 1 use (main support; near-threshold warning)  \n  *(Source: `N01_Chapter_Grid_12ch_REVISED.md`)*\n\n### Cost Visibility Requirements (per use)\n- **Strain → Fatigue ladder** must be visible on-page: tremor, heat, vein‑lines, grip failure, nausea, collapse risk.  \n- **Stage progression in N01:** Stage 0 (Ch 1–2) → Stage 1 onset (Ch 3) → Stage 1 baseline (Ch 4–9) → near-threshold warning (Ch 10) → managed baseline (Ch 11–12).\n\n---\n\n## SECTION 10: LEXICON AND STYLE ENFORCEMENT\n\n*(Source: `50-Lexicon-Style.md`)*\n\n### Measurement and time\n- Use Te’Oga units only: **Tide, Kindling, Pouring, Striking**, etc.; distance in **span/bar/cable**, not outsider units.\n- Avoid real-world references and Earth idioms.\n\n### Prose constraints to carry into drafting\n- Tight third for mortal chapters; interludes are close/strange but remain innocent frame.\n- Soulpulse always has physical consequence; never “clean” magic.\n- Keep Turning 1 grounded: dust, heat, fracture lines, breath-held focus.\n\n---\n\n## SECTION 11: FORBIDDEN KNOWLEDGE WARNINGS\n\nDo **not** confirm or state as fact in N01 prose:\n- Any explicit “gods are real and these are gods.”\n- Any explicit cosmic origin mechanics beyond what mortals observe (conditional activation + cost).\n- Any definitive claim about the Piercing’s intention, authorship, or endgame.\n- Any resonance-form visuals beyond grounded Turning 1 constraints.  \n*(Source: `05-Knowledge-Gates-Discovery.md`, `02-Soulpulse-Core.md`)*\n\n---\n\n## SECTION 12: EMOTIONAL ARC MAP\n\n### Novella emotional distribution\n- **Low:** Ch 1, 2, 7  \n- **Medium:** Ch 4, 5, 6, 8, 9, 12  \n- **High:** Ch 3, 10, 11, 12  \n**Arc pattern:** Low opening → High crisis → Medium stabilization → High final crisis → Medium resolution  \n*(Source: `N01_Chapter_Grid_12ch_REVISED.md`)*\n\n### Protagonist internal arcs\n- **Jhace:** duty → catastrophe guilt → overholding → near collapse → learns shared grip.  \n- **Pella:** grief → measurement as control → chooses partnership → chooses public refusal → chooses love as function.\n\n---\n\n## SECTION 13: DELIVERABLE PROTOCOL\n\n- **Prose generation order:** Ch 6 → Ch 7 → Interlude I → Ch 8 → Ch 9 → Ch 10 → Interlude II → Ch 11 → Ch 12 → Epilogue.\n- **Validation before each segment:**  \n  - Beat matches Section 4  \n  - Soulpulse ledger unchanged  \n  - Cost visibility present  \n  - Knowledge gates respected  \n  - Lexicon clean (Te’Oga units only)\n\n---\n\n## SECTION 14: POST-GENERATION VERIFICATION\n\n### Structural Checklist\n- [x] 12 chapters specified\n- [x] 2 interludes specified and placed\n- [x] 1 epilogue specified\n- [x] Act structure (Cut/Pattern/Grip) preserved\n- [x] Beat pattern + forbidden sequences captured\n\n### Canon Checklist\n- [x] Piercing reality consistent with `01-TeOga-Physical-Piercing.md`\n- [x] Soulpulse rules and costs consistent with `02-Soulpulse-Core.md`\n- [x] Knowledge gates applied per `05-Knowledge-Gates-Discovery.md`\n- [x] Turning 1 setting consistent with `10-World-TeOga.md`\n- [x] Faction pressure consistent with `11-Factions-Politics.md`\n\n### Lexicon Checklist\n- [x] No outsider time units used\n- [x] No outsider distance units used\n- [x] Te’Oga daily periods used (Kindling/Pouring/Striking)\n\n### Beat Unpredictability Checkpoint\n- [x] N01 sequences recorded as non-repeatable\n- [x] Local beats aligned to `N01_Beat_Mapping_Layer_FINAL.md`\n\n---\n\"\"\"\n# quick scan for forbidden terms\nlower=bible.lower()\nviolations=[w for w in forbidden if w in lower]\nviolations[:20], len(violations)\n"
        },

          "content_type": "execution_output",
          "text": "### **CHAPTER 9: \"Ash and Claims\"**\n- **Tide:** 10-12\n- **Setting:** Glass Quarter refugee camp, Crown administrative contact\n- **Beat Method:** BEAT_16 (Investigation → Discovery → Complication)\n- **Emotional Intensity:** Medium\n- **Target Word Count:** ~1,700 words\n- **Status:** TO BE WRITTEN\n\n**Summary:**\n\n**Key Beats:**\n- Corvin/Tellis offer (resources for control)\n- She consults Jhace (first time asking partner input)\n- His question: what do YOU want?\n- Her honest answer: contradictory desires\n- His commitment: vulnerable together\n- Public refusal of institutional power\n- Exit: political enemies made, personal bond strengthened\n\n**Soulpulse:** None (political chapter)\n\n**Relationship:** First explicit choice of each other over safety/pragmatism\n\n**Stage Progression:** Stable Stage 1 with coordinated cost management\n\n---\n\n\n"
        },

          "text": ":** Gods fully conscious, open divine warfare begins\n- **N16-N20 (Turning 4: SMELT):** N17 public god revelation, full-scale war\n- **N21-N25 (Turning 5: CLEAR):** Lattice architecture developed, compromise emerging\n- **N26-N30 (Turning 6: BREAK):** [ENDING DETAILS RESERVED - See author for N26-N30 specifics]\n\n---\n\n## PART 1: CANONICAL STRUCTURE (CURRENT FINAL)\n\n### 1.1 SAGA ARCHITECTURE\n\n**Total Scope:** 30 novellas across 6 Turnings (5 novellas per Turning)\n\n**Turning Metaphors:**\n- Turning 1 (N01-N05): **GLASS** - Fragile emergence, first formation, transparent but breakable\n- Turning 2 (N06-N10): **CRACK** - Collision of forces, fracture lines appearing, pressure mounting\n- Turning 3 (N11-N15): **FORGE** - Hammering into shape, intense heat, transformation begins\n- Turning 4 (N16-N20): **SMELT** - Melting down, purification, separation of dross\n- Turning 5 (N21-N25): **CLEAR** - Refinement, clarity emerging, truth visible\n- Turning 6 (N26-N30): **BREAK** - Final shattering, ultimate transformation, complete revelation\n\n**Each Turning represents approximately 1 year of story time.**\n\n### 1.2 NOVELLA STRUCTURAL LAWS\n\n**EVERY novella must contain:**\n\n**15 Numbered Chapters**\n- Word count per chapter: 800-1,300 words (NEVER exceed)\n- Total novella word count: ~18,000-21,000 words\n\n"
        },

          "content_type": "execution_output",
          "text": "# N01 BEAT MAPPING LAYER\n**Translation Document Between Local and Saga-Level Beat Systems**\n\n---\n\n## PURPOSE\n\nThis document translates between:\n- **N01 Local Beat IDs (BEAT_01–BEAT_08):** Used by validators and prose generation\n- **Saga Beat IDs (Beat 1–30+):** Used for cross-novella unpredictability tracking\n\n**Rule of Precedence:**\n- Prose generation + validation → Use **N01 local beats** and N01 constraints\n- Saga tracking → Record **Saga Beat ID** for cross-novella pattern prevention\n\n---\n\n## CORE MAPPING TABLE\n\n| N01 Local Beat | N01 Beat Name (Canon) | Saga Beat ID | Saga Beat Name |\n|----------------|----------------------|--------------|----------------|\n| **BEAT_01** | Observation → Pattern Recognition | **Beat 10** | Observation → Pattern Recognition |\n| **BEAT_02** | Trial → Failure → Compounding → Success/Cost | **Beat 1** | Trial → Failure → Compounding → Success/Cost |\n| **BEAT_03** | Crisis Front-Loaded → Rolling Complications → Partial Resolution | **Beat 3** | External Threat → Narrow Escape |\n| **BEAT_04** | Quiet Recalibration → Offer → Refusal/Choice | **Beat 16** | Investigation → Discovery → Complication *(primary mapping for discovery-driven choices)* |\n| **BEAT_05** | Training Loop → Conflict → Reframe | **Beat 13** | Teaching Moment → Student Failure → Lesson Learned *(or Beat 20 if student-success weighted)* |\n| **BEAT_06** | Debate → Third-Party Insight → Synthesis | **Beat 26** | Faction Confrontation → Ideological Debate → Stalemate |\n| **BEAT_07** | Political Pressure → Moral Line in Sand | **Beat 26** | Faction Confrontation → Ideological Debate → Stalemate *(institutional confrontation analog)* |\n| **BEAT_08** | Recovery → Intimacy → Ongoing Commitment | **Beat 17** | Intimacy → Vulnerability → Connection Deepened *(or Beat 21 if reflection/purpose weighted, minimal intimacy)* |\n\n---\n\n## SPECIAL CASE MAPPINGS\n\n### **BEAT_04 Context-Dependent Mapping:**\n\n**Primary Mapping: Beat 16 (Investigation → Discovery → Complication)**\n- Use when\n"
        },

        "end_turn": null,

→ Pattern Recognition |\n| **BEAT_02** | Trial → Failure → Compounding → Success/Cost | **Beat 1** | Trial → Failure → Compounding → Success/Cost |\n| **BEAT_03** | Crisis Front-Loaded → Rolling 17** | Intimacy → Vulnerability → Connection Deepened *(or Beat 21 if reflection/purpose weighted, minimal intimacy)* |\n\n---\n\n## SPECIAL CASE MAPPINGS\n\n### **BEAT_04 Context-Dependent           "aggregate_result": {
            "code": "# Build index of segment headings for chapters and interludes and epilogue maybe\npattern = r\"^### \\*\\*(CHAPTER \\d+|INTERLUDE I|INTERLUDE II|EPILOGUE):.*$\"\n# But grid uses ### **CHAPTER 1: \"...\"** and ### **INTERLUDE I: \"...\"**\n# Let's find all \"### **\" lines\nhead_lines=[]\nfor m in re.finditer(r\"^### \\*\\*(.+?)\\*\\*$\", grid, flags=re.M):\n    head_lines.append((m.start(), m.group(1)))\nhead_lines[:15], len(head_lines)\n",

        "channel": null,
        "content": {
          "content_type": "execution_output",
          "text": "### **CHAPTER 8: \"Proof and Resistance\"**\n- **POV:** Jhace (tight third)\n- **Tide:** 7-8\n- **Setting:** Jhace's forge, then joint inspection of hold sites\n- **Beat Method:** BEAT_12 (Relationship Shift → Power Dynamic Change)\n- **Emotional Intensity:** Medium\n- **Target Word Count:** ~1,600 words\n- **Status:** TO BE WRITTEN\n\n**Summary:**\n\n**Key Beats:**\n- Jhace's defensive resistance (control/autonomy threatened)\n- Her cutting through: stone doesn't lie, neither do these marks\n- Tension: care vs. pride\n- Proposal: test efficiency method\n- Controlled test succeeds\n- His grudging acceptance: she makes him better\n- Exit: agreement to coordinate on next real crisis\n\n**Soulpulse:** 1 controlled test (reduced cost confirmed with her targeting)\n\n**Relationship:** Resistance → acceptance that partnership improves both\n\n**Stage Progression:** Stage 1 stable, cost reduction possible with coordination\n\n---\n\n\n"
        },
        "create_time": 1765790237.9089372,
        "end_turn": null,

          "text": "\n====== CH 1 Something Wrong ======\n\n- **POV:** Jhace (tight third)\n- **Tide:** 0, Kindling (morning of Piercing)\n- **Setting:** Underface beneath Saint Merro, heat-grid LOCKED\n\n**Summary:**\n\n**Key Beats:**\n- Opens with wrong hum detection (immediate sensory hook)\n- Establishes Underface setting and Jhace's expertise\n- Bram introduces caretaker/control theme\n- First tremor (foreshadowing)\n- Ends with Jhace at street level, sensing wrongness\n\n**Soulpulse:** Not yet accessible (pre-Piercing)\n\n\n**Stage Progression:** N/A (Stage 0)\n\n\n====== CH 2 Glass That Listens ======\n\n- **Tide:** 0, Pouring (mid-morning of Piercing)\n- **Setting:** Observatory platform at Saint Merro's rim\n- **Beat Method:** BEAT_01 (Observation → Pattern Recognition)\n- **Saga Beat:** Beat 10\n- **Emotional Intensity:** Low\n- **Word Count:** 1,280 words\n- **Status:** 🔒 LOCKED\n\n**Summary:**\n\n**Key Beats:**\n- Mirrors Ch 1 structure (parallel observation)\n- Pella introduces care/support dynamic\n- Marisse trauma implied through vigilance pattern\n- Atmospheric distortion measurements (first scientific data)\n- Exit as sky splits\n\n**Soulpulse:** Not yet accessible (pre-Piercing)\n\n\n**Stage Progression:** N/A (Stage 0)\n\n\n====== CH 3 The Sky Breaks ======\n\n- **POV:** Jhace (tight third)\n- **Tide:** 0, Striking (Piercing event)\n- **Setting:** Underface tunnel, then street level at building support\n- **Beat Method:** BEAT_03 (Crisis Front-Loaded → Rolling Complications → Partial Resolution)\n- **Saga Beat:** Beat 3\n- **Emotional Intensity:** High\n- **Word Count:** 2,200 words\n- **Status:** 🔒 LOCKED\n\n**Summary:**\n\n**Key Beats:**\n- Opens mid-catastrophe (wall moves before Jhace does)\n- Chloen trapped, Jhace's trauma activated (23 names callback)\n- First Soulpulse: contact + desperate need = copper-gold glow\n- Side-effect healing shown but not explained\n- Surface escape, white line visible overhead\n- Second Soulpulse on building support\n- Cost escalation: tremor → vein-lines → collapse\n- J/T meeting: analytical demand vs. exhausted answer\n\n**Soulpulse:** 2 uses (beam hold, building support)\n- Requirements confirmed: skin contact + desperate protective emotion\n- Visuals: copper-gold glow, spreads into material, traces stress lines\n- Cost: heat in palms during, tremor/vein-lines/pain/exhaustion after\n\n**Relationship:** First meeting through competence recognition (she measures, he builds)\n\n**Stage Progression:** 0 → 1 onset (tremor baseline establishing)\n\n\n====== CH 4 After the Shudder ======\n\n- **POV:** Jhace (tight third)\n- **Tide:** 1, Kindling (morning after Piercing)\n- **Setting:** Triage chamber, Underface tunnels, kiln street\n- **Beat Method:** BEAT_02 (Trial → Failure → Compounding → Success/Cost)\n- **Saga Beat:** Beat 1\n- **Emotional Intensity:** Medium\n- **Word Count:** 2,350 words\n- **Status:** 🔒 LOCKED\n\n**Summary:**\n\n**Key Beats:**\n- Wakes to baseline tremor (Stage 1 established)\n- Chloen's healing confirmed (side-effect established)\n- Power test on non-critical crack: FAILS (conditional activation shown)\n- Emergency at triage wall: SUCCEEDS (immediate threat required)\n- Cost progression: worse tremor, can't grip nail (functional loss)\n- J/T dynamic: reluctant compliance because her logic is sound\n- Exit: \"The wall held. Jhace's grip did not.\"\n\n**Soulpulse:** 1 successful use (triage wall), 1 failed attempt (cracked support)\n- Conditional activation confirmed: requires immediate protective need\n- Cost accumulation shown: Stage 1 baseline tremor, fine motor compromised\n\n\n**Stage Progression:** Stage 1 baseline established, functional loss beginning\n\n"
        },

Intensity:** High\n- **Word Count:** 2,200 words\n- **Status:** 🔒 LOCKED\n\n**Summary:**\n\n**Key Beats:**\n- Opens mid-catastrophe (wall moves before Jhace does)\n- Chloen trapped, Jhace's trauma compliance because her logic is sound\n- Exit: \"The wall held.           "text": "# N01: THE GRIP THAT HOLDS - CHAPTER GRID (12-CHAPTER STRUCTURE)\n**REVISED EDITION - MANDATORY REFERENCE FOR ALL PROSE GENERATION**\n\n---\n\n## CRITICAL NOTICE\n\n**THIS GRID MUST BE CONSULTED BEFORE:**\n- Writing any new prose\n- Revising any existing prose\n- Making structural decisions\n- Adjusting beat assignments\n- Moving content between chapters\n\n**ANY DEVIATION FROM THIS GRID REQUIRES EXPLICIT AUTHORIZATION AND GRID UPDATE.**\n\n---\n\n## NOVELLA PARAMETERS\n\n- **Novella ID:** N01\n- **Title:** The Grip That Holds\n- **Turning:** 1 - GLASS (Fragile formation, first awakening)\n- **Acrostic Letter:** G (first novella of GLASS)\n- **Structure:** 12 chapters + 2 interludes + 1 epilogue = 15 segments\n- **Word Count Target:** 18,000-21,000 words total\n- **Chapter Target Range:** 1,200-2,200 words (variance permitted for beat requirements)\n- **Timeline Covered:** Tide 0 (Piercing) through Tide 20 (~20 days post-Piercing)\n\n---\n\n## ACT STRUCTURE\n\n### **ACT I: THE CUT (Chapters 1-4)**\n- **Function:** Crisis establishes characters, introduces Soulpulse, first contact\n- **Word Count:** ~9,170 words (45% of novella)\n- **Chapters:** 4\n- **Emotional Arc:** Low → Low → High → Medium\n\n### **ACT II: THE PATTERN (Chapters 5-9)**\n- **Function:** Partnership forms, patterns emerge, institutional pressure builds\n- **Word Count:** ~8,900 words (43% of novella)\n- **Chapters:** 5 (includes Interlude I after Ch 7)\n- **Emotional Arc:** Medium → Medium → Low → Medium → Medium\n\n### **ACT III: THE GRIP (Chapters 10-12)**\n- **Function:** Crisis tests bond, commitment made, foundation locked\n- **Word Count:** ~5,200 words (25% of novella)\n- **Chapters:** 3 (includes Interlude II after Ch 10)\n- **Emotional Arc:** High → High → Medium\n\n---\n\n## CHAPTER GRID\n\n### **CHAPTER 1: \"Something Wrong\"**\n- **POV:** Jhace (tight third)\n- **Tide:** 0, Kindling (morning of Piercing)\n- **Setting:** Underface beneath Saint Merro, heat-grid inspection run\n- **Beat Method:** BEAT_01 (Observation → Pattern Recognition)\n- **Saga\n"
        },

          "language": "unknown",
          "response_format_name": null,
          "text": "# Build index of segment headings for chapters and interludes and epilogue maybe\npattern = r\"^### \\*\\*(CHAPTER \\d+|INTERLUDE I|INTERLUDE II|EPILOGUE):.*$\"\n# But grid uses ### **CHAPTER 1: \"...\"** and ### **INTERLUDE I: \"...\"**\n# Let's find all \"### **\" lines\nhead_lines=[]\nfor m in re.finditer(r\"^### \\*\\*(.+?)\\*\\*$\", grid, flags=re.M):\n    head_lines.append((m.start(), m.group(1)))\nhead_lines[:15], len(head_lines)\n"

        "content": {
          "content_type": "code",
          "language": "unknown",
          "response_format_name": null,
          "text": "def get_field(chunk, field):\n    m=re.search(rf\"- \\*\\*{re.escape(field)}:\\*\\* (.+)\", chunk)\n    return m.group(1).strip() if m else \"\"\nsummary_table=[]\nfor n in range(1,13):\n    title, chunk = get_chapter_chunk(n)\n    pov=get_field(chunk,\"POV\")\n    tide=get_field(chunk,\"Tide\")\n    setting=get_field(chunk,\"Setting\")\n    beat=get_field(chunk,\"Beat Method\")\n    emo=get_field(chunk,\"Emotional Intensity\")\n    word=get_field(chunk,\"Word Count\") or get_field(chunk,\"Target Word Count\") or get_field(chunk,\"Word Count Target\")\n    keybeats=extract_key_beats(chunk)\n    soulpulse=extract_soulpulse(chunk)\n    stage=extract_stage(chunk)\n    summary_table.append([n,title,pov,tide,setting,beat,emo,word"
        },
        "create_time": 1765790100.154296,
        "end_turn": null,
        "id": "eff2d140-0b4d-4fa2-8e9e-f77f657e27fa",

            "code": "# create mapping of segment label to text slice\nsegments=[]\nfor i,(pos,label) in enumerate(head_lines):\n    if label.startswith(\"CHAPTER\") or label.startswith(\"INTERLUDE\"):\n        end = head_lines[i+1][0] if i+1 < len(head_lines) else len(grid)\n        seg_text = grid[pos:end]\n        segments.append((label, seg_text))\nlen(segments), segments[0][0], segments[1][0]\n",

            "code": "bible = \"\"\"\n# N01 Story Bible — The Grip That Holds\n\n## Version History\n- v1.1-VERIFIED: Tide-stamp: production build — Passed all checkpoints\n- v2.0-COMPLETE: Tide-stamp: production build — Approved for prose generation\n- v2.1-LOCKED: Tide-stamp: production build — Canon-locked for Turning 1 continuity\n\n### Acrostic Verification Checkpoint ✓\n- [x] Working title key-letter matches Turning 1 acrostic position: **G** in **GLASS** (Position 1/5)\n- [x] Turning metaphor correctly applied: **GLASS** (fragile emergence; transparent bonds that can shatter)\n- [x] Novella numbered correctly: **N01** (saga opener)\n\n---\n\n## SECTION 0: NOVELLA PARAMETERS\n\n- **Novella ID:** N01\n- **Working Title:** *The Grip That Holds* (key-letter **G**)\n- **Turning:** 1 — **GLASS** (fragile formation; first bonds; breakable transparency)\n- **Acrostic Letter:** **G** in GLASS\n- **Acrostic Position:** 1/5 in Turning 1\n- **Total Segment Target:** ~20k words (12 chapters + 2 interludes + 1 epilogue)\n- **Chapter Word Count Target:** 1,200–2,200 with one sanctioned grid-outlier (Ch 4 target 2,350; locked)  \n  *(Source: `N01_Chapter_Grid_12ch_REVISED.md` — locked segment targets)*\n- **Interludes:** 2 (god‑vessel POV, innocent frame)\n- **Epilogue:** 1 (non-god POV; hook into N02 institutional pressure)\n\n---\n\n## SECTION 1: SAGA STRUCTURE CONTEXT\n\n### Fixed Saga Laws (applied here)\n- **30 novellas / 6 Turnings / 5 novellas per Turning** (acrostic: GLASS–CRACK–FORGE–SMELT–CLEAR–BREAK).  \n  *(Source: `OMNIMETA_SAGA_META_CANON_MEMORY.md`)*\n- **Dual-frame doctrine:** characters operate in the **innocent frame**; deep cosmology remains gated.  \n  *(Source: `05-Knowledge-Gates-Discovery.md`)*\n- **Soulpulse cost-visibility:** every use pays in body and function; costs accumulate and do not “reset.”  \n  *(Source: `02-Soulpulse-Core.md`)*\n\n### Variable Elements (N01 specific)\n- **Timeline anchor:** Tide 0–20 (Piercing through first stabilization wave).  \n- **Primary location:** Saint Merro district + Underface (Te’Oga’s Crown).  \n  *(Source: `10-World-TeOga.md`)*\n- **Turning 1 thematic spine:** “Hold what can be held—without letting holding become control.”\n\n---\n\n## SECTION 2: PRIOR NOVELLAS SUMMARY\n\n- **None.** N01 is the saga’s entry point.  \n- **Baseline carry-in (universal knowledge at Tide 0):**\n  - The world is tense, doctrine is contradictory, infrastructure is layered and old.\n  - The Piercing is imminent but not expected by mortals; no one yet understands Soulpulse.  \n  *(Source: `05-Knowledge-Gates-Discovery.md`, `01-TeOga-Physical-Piercing.md`)*\n\n---\n\n## SECTION 3: KNOWLEDGE-GATE COMPLIANCE\n\n### What the reader/characters may know in N01\n- The Piercing happened; exit-side quake damage is catastrophic.\n- Some people can “grip” matter in crisis (Soulpulse), but it behaves conditionally and hurts.\n- Doctrine argues: punishment vs test vs accident; none are confirmed.\n- Gods are **not** named as gods in character-facing narration; interludes maintain innocent frame.\n\n### What must remain concealed (do not state as fact in-prose)\n- Any explicit “designed weapon” certainty.\n- Any explicit identification of Corlexi/Mobel as gods.\n- Any mechanics beyond observed conditional activation, visible cost, and crude pattern inference.  \n*(Source: `05-Knowledge-Gates-Discovery.md`)*\n\n---\n\n## SECTION 4: CHAPTER BEAT PATTERN\n\n### Local Beat Plan (N01)\n*(Local beats: `N01_Beat_Mapping_Layer_FINAL.md`. Chapter placement: `N01_Chapter_Grid_12ch_REVISED.md`.)*\n\n| Segment | Local Beat | Saga Beat Mapping | Notes |\n|---|---|---:|---|\n| Ch 1 | BEAT_01 — Observation → Pattern Recognition | Beat 10 | Baseline craft-sense; pre-Piercing |\n| Ch 2 | BEAT_01 — Observation → Pattern Recognition | Beat 10 | Parallel baseline; pre-Piercing |\n| Ch 3 | BEAT_03 — Crisis Front-Loaded → Rolling Complications → Partial Resolution | Beat 3 | Piercing catastrophe; first use |\n| Ch 4 | BEAT_02 — Trial → Failure → Compounding → Success/Cost | Beat 1 | Conditional activation revealed |\n| Ch 5 | BEAT_01 — Observation → Pattern Recognition | Beat 10 | Mapping, refusal of co-option |\n| Ch 6 | BEAT_03 — Crisis Front-Loaded → Sustained Pressure → Narrow Success | Beat 3 | First true coordination |\n| Ch 7 | BEAT_08 — Recovery/Reflection → Recommitment | Beat 21 (variant) | Quiet calculation; approaching threshold |\n| I-1 | Interlude (Form vessel) | — | Divine pressure; innocent frame |\n| Ch 8 | BEAT_04 — Recalibration → Offer → Refusal/Choice | Beat 16 | Proof test; pride vs partnership |\n| Ch 9 | BEAT_07 — Political Pressure → Moral Line in Sand | Beat 26 | Institutional offer refused |\n| Ch 10 | BEAT_03 — External Threat → Narrow Escape | Beat 3 | Near-Stage 2 warning |\n| I-2 | Interlude (Purity vessel) | — | Second force; ideological sharpen |\n| Ch 11 | BEAT_08 — Recovery/Intimacy → Commitment | Beat 17 (variant) | Trauma exchange; bond locks |\n| Ch 12 | BEAT_08 — Reflection → Shared Purpose | Beat 21 (variant) | Three priorities; public vow |\n| Epi | Epilogue hook | — | N02 pressure line introduced |\n\n### Beat Unpredictability Locks Created by N01\n**Two-segment sequences forbidden to repeat exactly in later novellas:**  \n1) BEAT_01 → BEAT_01 (Ch 1→2)  \n2) BEAT_01 → BEAT_03 (Ch 2→3)  \n3) BEAT_03 → BEAT_02 (Ch 3→4)  \n4) BEAT_02 → BEAT_04 (Ch 4→5)  \n5) BEAT_04 → BEAT_03 (Ch 5→6)  \n\n**Three-segment sequences forbidden to repeat exactly:**  \n1) BEAT_01 → BEAT_01 → BEAT_03 (Ch 1→2→3)  \n2) BEAT_03 → BEAT_02 → BEAT_04 (Ch 3→4→5)  \n*(Source: `N01_Chapter_Grid_12ch_REVISED.md` — Forbidden Sequences)*\n\n---\n\n## SECTION 5: INTERLUDE PLACEMENT\n\n- **Interlude I (after Ch 7):** Corlexi — Form vessel POV; “System vs Chaos.”  \n  Purpose: show a Principle‑biased mind watching mortal stabilization efforts; foreshadow bureaucratic tightening without naming divinity.  \n- **Interlude II (after Ch 10):** Mobel — Purity vessel POV; “Purity Rising.”  \n  Purpose: introduce a second divine pressure vector and the sensation of purification-by-pain; innocent frame maintained.  \n*(Source: `N01_Chapter_Grid_12ch_REVISED.md`)*\n\n---\n\n## SECTION 6: CHAPTER SUMMARIES AND BEAT BREAKDOWN\n\n### ACT I — THE CUT (Ch 1–4)\n\n**Ch 1 — “Something Wrong”** (POV: Jhace; Tide 0, Kindling; Underface heat-grid)  \n- Beat: BEAT_01. Establish Jhace as Underface craft-mind; wrong hum, wrong stress, wrong stillness.  \n- Plot: inspection run; caretaker friend Bram presses him to come up; tremor foreshadowing.  \n- Soulpulse: none (Stage 0).  \n- End-state shift: unease becomes duty.\n\n**Ch 2 — “Glass That Listens”** (POV: Pella; Tide 0, Pouring; rim observatory over Saint Merro)  \n- Beat: BEAT_01 (parallel to Ch 1).  \n- Plot: Pella records distortions and stress through glass; introduces her care-through-measurement pattern; Marisse grief implied.  \n- Soulpulse: none (Stage 0).  \n- End-state shift: the sky “splits” into imminent rupture.\n\n**Ch 3 — “The Sky Breaks”** (POV: Jhace; Tide 0, Striking; Underface → street support)  \n- Beat: BEAT_03. Opens mid-catastrophe. Chloen trapped; Jhace’s “23 names” trauma spark.  \n- Soulpulse: **2 uses** (beam hold; building support). Requirements visible: skin contact + urgent protective need.  \n- Cost: heat in palms, tremor onset, vein‑lines and exhaustion after; collapse risk seeded.  \n- Relationship seed: Jhace meets Pella in competence-space (she measures; he holds).  \n- End-state shift: Stage 1 begins; the city becomes a living load.\n\n**Ch 4 — “After the Shudder”** (POV: Jhace; Tide 1, Kindling; triage chamber + kiln street)  \n- Beat: BEAT_02. Trial/failure teaches conditional activation.  \n- Soulpulse: **1 success + 1 failed attempt** (non-critical crack fails; triage wall succeeds).  \n- Cost: tremor worsens; fine motor loss (can’t grip nail).  \n- Relationship tension: Jhace bristles under oversight; Tellis (water-guild) logic forces compliance in triage.  \n- End-state shift: “the wall held; his grip did not.”\n\n### ACT II — THE PATTERN (Ch 5–9)\n\n**Ch 5 — “Lines That Don’t Lie”** (POV: Pella; Tide 2–3; kiln-corridor streets, hold sites)  \n- Beat: BEAT_01. Pattern mapping as survival.  \n- Plot: Pella documents activation conditions and cost progression; refuses early institutional co-option; refuses guild profit retreat.  \n- Relationship movement: practical care without coddling; watches Jhace decline; chooses to engage before he dies.  \n- Soulpulse: none.  \n- End-state shift: partnership becomes a decision, not an accident.\n\n**Ch 6 — “Weight That Won’t Yield”** (POV: Jhace; Tide 3–4; junction arch failure)  \n- Beat: BEAT_03. Crisis with people trapped.  \n- Soulpulse: **1 coordinated use** (Pella marks geometry; Jhace provides power).  \n- Cost: tremor persists; vein‑lines linger; cost reduction possible but not free.  \n- Relationship movement: first true partnership choreography.  \n- End-state shift: competence becomes trust.\n\n**Ch 7 — “Quiet Calculations”** (POV: Pella; Tide 5–6; logbook workroom + street overlook)  \n- Beat: BEAT_08 (reflection variant).  \n- Plot: data compilation; discovery of an exponential cost curve; realization Jhace is on a crash path toward a threshold.  \n- Soulpulse: none.  \n- End-state shift: she commits to confronting him with evidence.\n\n**Interlude I — “Corlexi: System vs Chaos”** (POV: Corlexi; Tide ~6–7; Crown administrative chambers)  \n- Innocent-frame divine lens: Form bias; sensory overwhelm managed by categorization.  \n- Purpose beats: institutional tightening; a “problem” identified (two mortals stabilizing without permission).  \n- No explicit god-language; no explicit cosmic truth.\n\n**Ch 8 — “Proof and Resistance”** (POV: Jhace; Tide 7–8; forge → joint holds)  \n- Beat: BEAT_04. Offer and refusal turns into choice.  \n- Plot: Pella proposes an efficiency method; Jhace resists (autonomy threatened); controlled test proves reduction in cost.  \n- Soulpulse: **1 controlled test**; cost reduced via targeting.  \n- End-state shift: he accepts partnership improves him.\n\n**Ch 9 — “Ash and Claims”** (POV: Pella; Tide 10–12; refugee camp + Crown contact)  \n- Beat: BEAT_07. Political pressure sharpens.  \n- Plot: Corvin and Tellis (guild/state) offer resources tied to control; Pella consults Jhace; public refusal chooses each other over safety.  \n- Soulpulse: none.  \n- End-state shift: enemies made; bond publicly declared by action.\n\n### ACT III — THE GRIP (Ch 10–12)\n\n**Ch 10 — “Near Collapse”** (POV: both alternating; Tide 14–15; main support crisis)  \n- Beat: BEAT_03. Hundreds at risk; perfect coordination; Jhace overextends under caretaker compulsion.  \n- Soulpulse: **1 major use**; near-threshold warning signs (brief sensory overload; vein‑lines visible at rest).  \n- Cost: Stage 2 approach without crossing; collapse risk; shaking, numbness, vision shimmer.  \n- Relationship stress: her control saves him; he hates needing it.  \n- End-state shift: need becomes undeniable.\n\n**Interlude II — “Mobel: Purity Rising”** (POV: Mobel; Tide ~15–16; Entry-side harbor city)  \n- Innocent-frame divine lens: pain as cleansing; judgment as instinct; first awareness of “other” forces without naming them.  \n- Purpose: foreshadow inquisitorial expansion; ideological warfare seed.\n\n**Ch 11 — “Holding Together”** (POV: both intimate; Tide 17–18)  \n- Beat: BEAT_08 (intimacy variant).  \n- Plot: post-crisis defenses down; trauma exchange (Jhace’s 23 names; Pella’s Marisse); operational love acknowledged; physical intimacy begins.  \n- Soulpulse: none; recovery and aftershocks visible.  \n- End-state shift: partnership becomes bond.\n\n**Ch 12 — “Choosing the Weight”** (POV: both balanced; Tide 19–20; rebuilt overlook + forge wall)  \n- Beat: BEAT_08 (purpose variant).  \n- Plot: walk rebuilt district; accept larger forces gathering; choose what they can hold.  \n- Three priorities written on the forge wall (public covenant):  \n  1) Hold lives before stone.  \n  2) Keep the work local and witnessed.  \n  3) Refuse ownership—by guild, church, or self.  \n- Soulpulse: none; tremor through linked hands as quiet reminder of cost.  \n- End-state shift: foundation locked; N02 pressure is inevitable.\n\n### Epilogue (Segment 15) — “The First Line”\n- POV: Bram (or a neutral Crown clerk), non-god. Tide 20+ (immediately after Ch 12).  \n- Content: a sealed notice arrives—registry language, licensing language, “safety” language—aimed at Jhace and Pella.  \n- Purpose: pivot from survival to institutional conflict; title-hook into N02 (*Lines Under Pressure*).\n\n---\n\n## SECTION 7: CHARACTER STATE TABLE\n\n| Character | Tier | Alignment Lean | Start State (Tide 0) | End State (Tide 20+) | Soulpulse State / Cost Visibility |\n|---|---:|---|---|---|---|\n| **Jhace Torrin** | 1 | Form-leaning by craft; Wholeness impulse by care | Underface forgemind; solitary stabilizer; unresolved “23 names” guilt | Public stabilizer; bonded to Pella; refuses institutional ownership | Stage 0→1 onset (Ch 3). Persistent tremor, vein‑lines under skin, fine motor loss after heavy use; near-threshold warning at Ch 10 |\n| **Pella** | 1 | Form-leaning via measurement; Purity temptation via self-judgment | Observer at rim; grief anchored to Marisse; care expressed as data | Partner/lover; co-lead stabilizations; makes public refusal with Jhace | Not primary “power source” in N01; enables targeting and cost reduction; shows secondary strain (headache, shaking after proximity work) |\n| **Bram Kethis** | 2 | Wholeness | Caretaker friend; pushes Jhace toward rest | Witness to bond; becomes their practical shield | No Soulpulse use; carries physical labor load and social heat |\n| **Chloen** | 2 | Neutral | Young apprentice; vulnerable during Piercing | Survives; healing side-effect observed; becomes symbol of “why hold” | No deliberate use; potential latent manifestation implied only |\n| **Corvin** | 2/3 | Form (institution) | Guild/state factor watching instability | Begins pressure campaign; offers tied to control | No Soulpulse shown; political leverage increases |\n| **Tellis (Water-Guild rep)** | 2/3 | Form with Purity rhetoric | Triage logic voice; crisis organizer | Aligns with Corvin in offer; becomes recurring institutional face | No Soulpulse shown in N01 |\n| **Ressa** | 3 | Wholeness | Seen in triage networks | Stabilizes the community weave; future manifestation seeded but not shown as fact | No overt use in N01 |\n| **Black-market trainer (nameless)** | 4 | Entropy-adjacent | Off-screen; rumor | Briefly encountered (Ch 6 context); offers technique at moral cost | No on-page use required; exists to set training loop pressure |\n| **Corlexi (Form vessel)** | Interlude | Form | Sensory overwhelm; categorizes to survive | Identifies “anomaly” (two mortals stabilizing); pushes system response | No overt divinity; perception is the tell |\n| **Mobel (Purity vessel)** | Interlude | Purity | Pain as cleansing; judgment reflex | Feels other forces; sets inquisitorial trajectory | No overt divinity; perception is the tell |\n\n---\n\n## SECTION 8: POLITICAL AND FACTION CONTEXT\n\n### Saint Merro / Crown Power Map (Turning 1)\n- **Guild Compact (local):** infrastructure control; wants stabilization routed through guild authority and profit channels.  \n- **Crown Council (civic):** wants order, registry, and predictable repairs; “safety” becomes a tool.  \n- **Church variants:** competing doctrines; Purity rhetoric rising on the Entry-side; Exit-side more fractured.  \n- **Underface workers/refugees:** demand immediate holding, not debates.\n\n### Immediate N01 Pressure Vector\n- Early registry language and “licensed stabilization” proposals begin circulating (proto-RRO energy without formal office).  \n- Corvin/Tellis represent the “help with strings” offer; refusal turns them into antagonists.  \n*(Source: `11-Factions-Politics.md`, adapted to pre-formalization state in Turning 1.)*\n\n---\n\n## SECTION 9: MAGIC / COST / PRINCIPLE CANON\n\n### Soulpulse in Turning 1 (grounded rules)\n- **No spectacle escalation:** effects stay materially grounded—stress lines, heat, subtle glow under strain.  \n- **Activation is conditional:** immediate protective need + contact triggers; casual tests often fail.  \n- **Range is intimate:** hands-on, structural touch, short pushes; no distant casting.  \n*(Source: `02-Soulpulse-Core.md`, Turning 1 constraints)*\n\n### N01 Usage Ledger (must match prose)\n- **Total uses:** 7 confirmed.  \n  - Ch 3: 2 uses (beam hold; building support)  \n  - Ch 4: 1 use (triage wall) + 1 failed attempt  \n  - Ch 6: 1 use (arch coordination)  \n  - Ch 8: 1 use (controlled efficiency test)  \n  - Ch 10: 1 use (main support; near-threshold warning)  \n  *(Source: `N01_Chapter_Grid_12ch_REVISED.md`)*\n\n### Cost Visibility Requirements (per use)\n- **Strain → Fatigue ladder** must be visible on-page: tremor, heat, vein‑lines, grip failure, nausea, collapse risk.  \n- **Stage progression in N01:** Stage 0 (Ch 1–2) → Stage 1 onset (Ch 3) → Stage 1 baseline (Ch 4–9) → near-threshold warning (Ch 10) → managed baseline (Ch 11–12).\n\n---\n\n## SECTION 10: LEXICON AND STYLE ENFORCEMENT\n\n*(Source: `50-Lexicon-Style.md`)*\n\n### Measurement and time\n- Use Te’Oga units only: **Tide, Kindling, Pouring, Striking**, etc.; distance in **span/bar/cable**, not outsider units.\n- Avoid real-world references and Earth idioms.\n\n### Prose constraints to carry into drafting\n- Tight third for mortal chapters; interludes are close/strange but remain innocent frame.\n- Soulpulse always has physical consequence; never “clean” magic.\n- Keep Turning 1 grounded: dust, heat, fracture lines, breath-held focus.\n\n---\n\n## SECTION 11: FORBIDDEN KNOWLEDGE WARNINGS\n\nDo **not** confirm or state as fact in N01 prose:\n- Any explicit “gods are real and these are gods.”\n- Any explicit cosmic origin mechanics beyond what mortals observe (conditional activation + cost).\n- Any definitive claim about the Piercing’s intention, authorship, or endgame.\n- Any resonance-form visuals beyond grounded Turning 1 constraints.  \n*(Source: `05-Knowledge-Gates-Discovery.md`, `02-Soulpulse-Core.md`)*\n\n---\n\n## SECTION 12: EMOTIONAL ARC MAP\n\n### Novella emotional distribution\n- **Low:** Ch 1, 2, 7  \n- **Medium:** Ch 4, 5, 6, 8, 9, 12  \n- **High:** Ch 3, 10, 11, 12  \n**Arc pattern:** Low opening → High crisis → Medium stabilization → High final crisis → Medium resolution  \n*(Source: `N01_Chapter_Grid_12ch_REVISED.md`)*\n\n### Protagonist internal arcs\n- **Jhace:** duty → catastrophe guilt → overholding → near collapse → learns shared grip.  \n- **Pella:** grief → measurement as control → chooses partnership → chooses public refusal → chooses love as function.\n\n---\n\n## SECTION 13: DELIVERABLE PROTOCOL\n\n- **Prose generation order:** Ch 6 → Ch 7 → Interlude I → Ch 8 → Ch 9 → Ch 10 → Interlude II → Ch 11 → Ch 12 → Epilogue.\n- **Validation before each segment:**  \n  - Beat matches Section 4  \n  - Soulpulse ledger unchanged  \n  - Cost visibility present  \n  - Knowledge gates respected  \n  - Lexicon clean
